anomander rake.name: Anomander Rake
anomander rake.description: Son of Mother Dark, Lord of Moon's Spawn, Knight of High House Dark. He is the leader of the Tiste Andii, a brooding and ancient being who has lived for hundreds of thousands of years. He wields Dragnipur, a soul-stealing sword forged by Draconus that traps the souls of those it kills inside a pocket dimension where they must eternally pull a massive wagon to keep Darkness ahead of Chaos. Rake is a Soletaken capable of taking the form of an enormous black dragon. He is considered one of the most powerful beings in the world. He first appears defending the city of Pale from the Malazan Empire from atop Moon's Spawn, a floating mountain fortress. Despite his terrifying power, Rake is deeply compassionate and carries an immense burden of responsibility for his people, who have largely lost their will to live after millennia of existence. His final act is one of the most pivotal events in the series, sacrificing himself deliberately to allow Dragnipur to be shattered, thereby freeing Mother Dark and healing the ancient rift between the Tiste Andii and their goddess. He is tall, dark-skinned with long silver-white hair, and his eyes shift colour depending on his mood, with flat black being the most dangerous.

whiskeyjack.name: Whiskeyjack
whiskeyjack.description: Born Jedden, he is a legendary Malazan soldier who rose through the ranks during the Emperor's campaigns. He was once a commander-level officer but was deliberately demoted to sergeant by Empress Laseen as part of her purge of the Old Guard loyalists. He commands the Bridgeburner squad sent into Darujhistan. He is a pragmatic, deeply respected leader whose soldiers would follow him anywhere. He walks with a limp from a badly healed leg injury that could have been treated but was neglected as part of Laseen's subtle persecution. He falls in love with the Tiste Andii Korlat during the Pannion War campaign. He is promoted back to overall command during the alliance with Caladan Brood and Dujek but is tragically killed during the battle at Coral when his bad leg gives out at a critical moment and Kallor strikes him down. His death is one of the most devastating moments in the series and deeply affects everyone who knew him. The surviving Bridgeburners carry his memory as an almost sacred thing.

ganoes paran.name: Ganoes Paran
ganoes paran.description: The eldest son of a noble Malazan family from Unta, he grows up romanticising soldiers and the military life. As a young officer he witnesses the destruction of his unit in Itko Kan by the possessed girl Sorry (Apsalar) and is assigned to the Bridgeburners by Adjunct Lorn as a captain, though the squad distrusts him initially. He is stabbed through the heart by the puppet Hairlock early on but is revived by Oponn's intervention, gaining a connection to the Deck of Dragons. Over the course of the series he grows from a naive noble into a world-weary but capable leader. He eventually becomes the Master of the Deck of Dragons, a role that gives him authority over the houses of power and the ability to travel between warrens. He plays a critical role in the final convergence, using his unique position to help orchestrate events. He has two sisters, Tavore and Felisin, and carries guilt over not protecting Felisin from the otataral mines. He is thoughtful, self-doubting, and grows increasingly comfortable wielding enormous metaphysical power responsibly.

tattersail.name: Tattersail
tattersail.description: A large, warm-hearted woman and powerful cadre mage serving with the Bridgeburners at the Siege of Pale. She is a practitioner of Thyr, the Warren of Light. During the disastrous battle at Pale she witnesses the betrayal by High Mage Tayschrenn that kills several of her fellow mages. She enters into a brief love affair with Ganoes Paran. She is killed by Adjunct Lorn and Onos T'oolan but her soul, along with those of Nightchill and Bellurdan, is reborn through a Rhivi ritual into the child who becomes Silverfox. She is remembered as compassionate and perceptive, one of the few mages who genuinely cared about the common soldiers.

quick ben.name: Quick Ben
quick ben.description: Born Ben Adaephon Delat, he is the Bridgeburner squad mage and one of the most cunning and mysterious characters in the series. He has access to at least twelve warrens simultaneously, which is virtually unheard of. The secret to his power is that he absorbed the souls of eleven other mages from a Holy Falah cult during a desperate ritual in Seven Cities. He is from the Dal Honese people and is dark-skinned, thin, and wiry. He is best friends with Kalam and the two of them have a long history together predating their time in the Bridgeburners. He is constantly scheming and planning multiple steps ahead, often keeping his plans secret even from allies. He plays critical roles in nearly every major campaign, from Darujhistan to Black Coral to the final march to Kolanse. He has a confrontation with Icarium that reveals the terrifying extent of his power. Despite his seemingly callous scheming he is deeply loyal to his friends and genuinely working toward the greater good.

kalam mekhar.name: Kalam Mekhar
kalam mekhar.description: A huge, dark-skinned assassin of Seven Cities origin, formerly a member of the Claw before defecting to the Bridgeburners. He is one of the deadliest killers alive, capable of fighting his way through entire teams of Claw operatives. He and Quick Ben are inseparable friends with a bond forged over years of shared danger. He is sent on a mission to assassinate Empress Laseen, travelling the breadth of Seven Cities alone, during which he demonstrates his extraordinary combat skills in several legendary fight sequences. He is conflicted about the rebellion in his homeland, understanding the rebels' grievances but unwilling to support the Whirlwind's religious fanaticism. He eventually reconciles with Laseen after she demonstrates the depth of the threats facing the Empire. He marries Minala and helps protect the children of the Shadow Realm. He joins the Bonehunters for the final campaign and plays a key role in events at Malaz City.

fiddler.name: Fiddler
fiddler.description: Originally called Fiddler for his love of playing the fiddle, he serves as the Bridgeburners' sapper (explosives specialist). He later uses the alias Strings when he rejoins the military under the Bonehunters. He is one of the emotional hearts of the entire series, a common soldier whose perspective grounds the narrative through immense cosmic events. He has a talent for reading the Deck of Dragons that he finds deeply unsettling and tries to avoid. He is fiercely loyal to his comrades and carries the trauma of losing the original Bridgeburners heavily. He is best friends with Hedge, and their sapper partnership is legendary. He plays fiddle at key moments in the series and his music has a supernatural quality, drawing the attention of gods. He survives the entire series, one of the few original Bridgeburners to do so, and is present at the final battle against the Forkrul Assail. He is short, plain-faced, and unremarkable in appearance but possessed of an extraordinary depth of courage and compassion.

apsalar.name: Apsalar
apsalar.description: Originally a young fisher-girl from Itko Kan named Sorry, she was possessed by the god Cotillion who used her body as a tool for assassination, including killing members of Ganoes Paran's unit. After Cotillion is driven out of her, she retains all of his assassin skills and memories but reclaims her own identity. She travels with Crokus Younghand, with whom she develops a complicated relationship. She eventually takes on assassination missions independently, becoming one of the deadliest killers in the world while remaining a surprisingly empathetic person. She struggles with the tension between who she was and the lethal skills that were forced upon her. She returns to serve Cotillion voluntarily in later books, working to neutralise threats in Seven Cities. Her relationship with Crokus ultimately ends as she recognises that her path is too dangerous to share.

crokus younghand.name: Crokus Younghand
crokus younghand.description: A young thief from Darujhistan who becomes embroiled in the Malazan Empire's designs on his city when he accidentally steals a coin that has been touched by Oponn. He is agile, impulsive, and driven by a youthful sense of adventure that gradually matures into something more hardened. He falls in love with Apsalar and travels with her to Seven Cities. Over time the innocent boy becomes the more cynical Cutter, a name he adopts to reflect his transformation. He struggles with his feelings for Apsalar, who increasingly moves beyond his ability to follow. He has connections to Rallick Nom and the Assassins' Guild of Darujhistan. By the later books he has become a capable fighter in his own right, though he carries a melancholy from everything he has lost.

kruppe.name: Kruppe
kruppe.description: A rotund, seemingly foolish man of Darujhistan who speaks in elaborate third-person and appears to be nothing more than a gluttonous buffoon. In reality he is one of the most powerful and enigmatic figures in the city, possibly in the world. He is an Elder Dreamer who walks the realm of dreams with impunity and has a direct connection to the Azath and to K'rul. He manipulates events in Darujhistan to protect the city from Malazan conquest, from the Pannion Domin, and from various other threats. He serves as the voice of Darujhistan itself in a metaphysical sense. He rides a mule that is possibly as ancient and supernatural as he is. Nobody, including gods, fully understands what Kruppe truly is, and he likes it that way. He is endlessly entertaining, endlessly eating pastries, and endlessly underestimated.

rallick nom.name: Rallick Nom
rallick nom.description: A professional assassin of the Darujhistan Guild who becomes embroiled in the city's political machinations. He loves Vorcan, the Guild's Mistress, and his actions are often driven by loyalty to her and to his city. He is touched by the Azath and spends time trapped in the Azath House of Darujhistan, emerging changed. He is skilled, disciplined, and one of the most dangerous fighters in the city. He works closely with Coll to expose the scheming of Councillor Turban Orr. He is quiet and intense, a man of few words who lets his actions speak.

murillio.name: Murillio
murillio.description: A handsome, charming swordsman and socialite of Darujhistan, friend of Kruppe, Rallick, Crokus, and Coll. He is a capable duellist but better known for his romantic affairs and his loyalty to his friends. He acts as a protector and mentor figure to the young Crokus. In later books he takes on the care of a young boy named Harllo and his story becomes increasingly tragic. He dies defending others in Darujhistan, a moment that is deeply moving because of how earnestly he lived his life despite his flaws.

coll.name: Coll
coll.description: A Darujhistan nobleman who was betrayed and lost his seat on the Council, falling into alcoholism before being helped by his friends Murillio and Rallick. He eventually reclaims his position and becomes an important political figure in the city. He is large, bluff, and more perceptive than he appears. He kills Councillor Turban Orr in a duel orchestrated by Rallick to restore justice.

baruk.name: Baruk
baruk.description: The High Alchemist of Darujhistan and de facto leader of the T'orrud Cabal, a secret group of powerful mages who protect the city. He is an Ascendant in his own right, having lived for centuries. He is cautious, wise, and genuinely dedicated to Darujhistan's independence. He makes a pact with Anomander Rake to defend the city and plays a crucial role in events there across multiple books. He maintains a network of spies and informants throughout the city and commands considerable sorcerous power.

vorcan.name: Vorcan
vorcan.description: Mistress of the Assassins' Guild in Darujhistan, she is an Ascendant of extraordinary skill. She attempts to assassinate the members of the T'orrud Cabal during the Malazan incursion into Darujhistan but is thwarted. She has a complicated relationship with Rallick Nom. She is one of the most dangerous individuals in the city, capable of killing virtually anyone, and operates with cold professional detachment.

serrat.name: Serrat
serrat.description: A Tiste Andii assassin in service to Anomander Rake, sent to Darujhistan on missions. She is competent and loyal but encounters interference from Crokus during her operations in the city. She represents the reach of Rake's power into urban affairs.

hairlock.name: Hairlock
hairlock.description: A Malazan cadre mage who is mortally wounded at the Siege of Pale and has his soul transferred into a wooden puppet by Quick Ben to preserve his knowledge. The transfer drives him increasingly insane and he becomes a dangerous, uncontrollable entity. He traverses the warrens in puppet form, gathering intelligence but growing more erratic and threatening. He is eventually destroyed when he becomes too dangerous to control.

tayschrenn.name: Tayschrenn
tayschrenn.description: High Mage of the Malazan Empire and advisor to the Empress. He is immensely powerful but widely distrusted by the military after the Siege of Pale, where his actions led to the deaths of several fellow mages in what appeared to be a deliberate betrayal. His true motivations are complex and he is eventually revealed to be working toward larger goals that require difficult sacrifices. He is tall, gaunt, and projects an aura of menace even when being cooperative.

dujek onearm.name: Dujek Onearm
dujek onearm.description: High Fist of the Malazan Empire, commander of Onearm's Host, the elite army that conquered much of Genabackis. He lost his arm in battle and replaced it with a stump. He is pragmatic, experienced, and deeply respected by his soldiers. He is forced to declare his army renegade as part of a scheme with Empress Laseen, secretly allying with Anomander Rake and Caladan Brood against the Pannion Domin. He represents the best of the Malazan military tradition — a soldier who genuinely cares about his troops and fights for the greater good. He is one of the few High Fists to survive the Empress's purges of the Old Guard.

toc the younger.name: Toc the Younger
toc the younger.description: A Malazan scout and soldier, son of the legendary commander Toc the Elder. He loses an eye to a fragment of Moon's Spawn at the Siege of Pale. He is swept into a warren and separated from the Malazan forces, eventually ending up in the territory of the Pannion Domin where he endures terrible suffering. He befriends the T'lan Imass warrior Tool and later the wolf gods. He is killed and resurrected multiple times throughout the series, each time carrying the trauma of his previous deaths. He is wry, brave, and deeply unfortunate.

tool (onos t'oolan).name: Tool (Onos T'oolan)
tool (onos t'oolan).description: A clanless T'lan Imass warrior, one of the undead Imass who underwent the Ritual of Tellann over three hundred thousand years ago. He serves the Malazan Empire initially and befriends Toc the Younger. He is eventually freed from the Ritual by the Eres'al and becomes mortal again, regaining flesh and emotion. He becomes war chief of the White Face Barghast. He is deeply honourable, philosophical about his long existence, and one of the most skilled warriors ever to have lived. His love for Toc and later for Hetan makes him vulnerable in ways the undead T'lan Imass could never be.

lorn.name: Lorn
lorn.description: Adjunct to Empress Laseen before Tavore, she is a competent and ruthless servant of the Empire. She is sent to Darujhistan with the mission of freeing the Jaghut Tyrant Raest from his barrow to destabilise the city. She carries an otataral sword that negates magic. She is killed during the events in Darujhistan. She is portrayed as someone who has sacrificed her humanity for duty and come to regret it too late.

adjunct tavore paran.name: Adjunct Tavore Paran
adjunct tavore paran.description: The middle Paran child who becomes Adjunct to the Empress after Lorn's death. She is cold, controlled, and seemingly emotionless, revealing her plans and motivations to no one. She leads the Bonehunters on an increasingly impossible campaign that culminates in the march across the Glass Desert to Kolanse. It gradually becomes clear that she has been working toward freeing the Crippled God — not destroying him — recognising that he is a being in torment who deserves compassion rather than further punishment. She may or may not know that she killed her own sister Felisin. She wields an otataral sword and is utterly without magical ability, commanding entirely through force of will and tactical brilliance. She is one of the most enigmatic characters in the series, never fully explained, and her soldiers follow her despite having no understanding of why they march.

felisin paran.name: Felisin Paran
felisin paran.description: The youngest Paran sibling, a teenage girl who is sent to the brutal otataral mines on Laseen's orders as part of a political purge of noble families. Her sister Tavore, who is behind the order, does not intervene. The mines destroy Felisin's innocence and she survives through prostitution and develops a bitter, self-destructive personality. She escapes and is groomed to become Sha'ik Reborn, the prophetess of the Whirlwind rebellion. She is consumed by hatred for her sister and a desire for revenge. She is eventually killed by Tavore herself, though Tavore does not realise whom she has struck down. Felisin's story is one of the most disturbing and tragic in the series, showing how institutional cruelty destroys human beings.

heboric light touch.name: Heboric Light Touch
heboric light touch.description: A defrocked priest of Fener and historian who lost his hands as punishment for his writings. He is sent to the otataral mines alongside Felisin. He is a grumbling, scholarly man whose body becomes a vessel for various supernatural forces throughout the series. His hands grow back as ghost hands that glow with jade light, connected to the Jade Strangers and the Crippled God. He is tattooed extensively and those tattoos carry power. He serves as a reluctant spiritual figure, carrying burdens of power he neither sought nor wanted.

baudin.name: Baudin
baudin.description: A Talon agent sent to the otataral mines to secretly protect Felisin Paran on behalf of her sister Tavore. He poses as a common thug and endures Felisin's contempt without revealing his true purpose. He is large, brutal in appearance, and utterly dedicated to his mission. He dies protecting Felisin, and she never learns that he was her guardian all along, making his sacrifice particularly bitter.

kulp.name: Kulp
kulp.description: A Malazan cadre mage who attempts to escape Seven Cities by sea during the Whirlwind rebellion. He becomes caught in a nightmare journey through a warren with Heboric, Felisin, and Baudin. He is competent and sympathetic but ultimately overwhelmed by the horrors he encounters. He dies during the crossing, consumed by a D'ivers.

duiker.name: Duiker
duiker.description: The Imperial Historian assigned to observe Coltaine's command in Seven Cities. He witnesses the entire Chain of Dogs, one of the most harrowing military retreats in the series, and his account of it forms the emotional core of the second book. He is a scholar turned reluctant warrior who is captured at the Fall of Coltaine and crucified along the road to Aren. He is later freed and carries the trauma of what he witnessed for the rest of his life. His perspective gives the reader access to the human cost of war in visceral detail.

coltaine.name: Coltaine
coltaine.description: A Wickan Fist who is given command of the Seventh Army in Seven Cities just as the Whirlwind rebellion erupts. He leads the Chain of Dogs, a months-long fighting retreat across hundreds of leagues of hostile territory, protecting tens of thousands of refugees while being pursued by vastly superior rebel forces. He is taciturn, brilliant tactically, and utterly devoted to his duty. He performs numerous battlefield miracles, using Wickan horse-warrior tactics to devastating effect. He is betrayed at the gates of Aren when the city's garrison refuses to open the gates, and he is captured and crucified on a convergence of roads. Wickan crows gather around his body, and the nature of his death carries supernatural significance. He is widely considered one of the greatest military leaders in the series.

bult.name: Bult
bult.description: Coltaine's elderly Wickan veteran and uncle. He is grizzled, tough, and fiercely loyal to his nephew. He serves as a bridge between Coltaine's Wickan ways and the Malazan military structure. He fights throughout the Chain of Dogs and dies at the Fall.

squint.name: Squint
squint.description: A Malazan archer who fires the mercy shot that kills Coltaine on the cross, sparing him further suffering. This single act defines his character and haunts him afterward. He represents the impossible choices war forces upon ordinary soldiers.

lull.name: Lull
lull.description: A Malazan captain during the Chain of Dogs, a grizzled marine officer who leads his troops through impossible circumstances with dark humour and stubborn determination. He dies during the Chain of Dogs campaign, one of many officers ground down by the relentless fighting.

keneb.name: Keneb
keneb.description: A Malazan Fist who survives the Seven Cities rebellion and joins the Bonehunters. He is a capable, level-headed officer who provides stability within Tavore's command structure. He is married and carries the weight of having survived when so many others did not. He rises to become one of Tavore's most trusted officers.

minala.name: Minala
minala.description: A woman who joins Kalam during his journey through Seven Cities. She is tough, capable, and becomes Kalam's lover and eventually wife. She later protects the children of the Shadow Realm, raising an army of orphans in service to Shadowthrone. She is fierce, practical, and devoted to the children under her care.

mappo runt.name: Mappo Runt
mappo runt.description: A Trell warrior sworn by his elders to accompany and restrain Icarium, preventing him from unleashing his destructive potential. He genuinely loves Icarium as a friend and is tormented by his secret role, which requires him to betray that friendship if Icarium ever becomes too dangerous. He is large, powerful, and carries a heavy leather sack containing various weapons. He is separated from Icarium against his will and spends much of the series desperately trying to find his way back. He is one of the most honourable and tragic figures in the story, caught between duty and love.

icarium.name: Icarium
icarium.description: A half-Jaghut, half-Jhag wanderer who suffers from profound memory loss. He is a gentle, curious scholar and tinkerer by nature, but when he loses control he becomes the most destructive single force on the planet. Entire civilisations have been destroyed by his rages and he has no memory of any of it. His companion Mappo Runt is sworn to keep him calm and to prevent his awakening. The tension between Icarium's gentle nature and his apocalyptic potential is one of the most poignant elements of the series. Various factions attempt to use him as a weapon. He builds mechanisms and inventions of extraordinary sophistication during his calm periods.

iskaral pust.name: Iskaral Pust
iskaral pust.description: High Priest of Shadow, an erratic and scheming old man who lives in a monastery in the Holy Desert of Raraku. He speaks his inner thoughts aloud, believing no one can hear his constant scheming, which provides rich comedy. He is genuinely devoted to Shadowthrone's plans despite his apparent incompetence. He rides a mule named after himself and is married to Mogora. Despite his absurdity he is magically powerful and plays important roles in several key events.

mogora.name: Mogora
mogora.description: Iskaral Pust's wife, a D'ivers who can split into hundreds of spiders. She and Pust have a viciously antagonistic marriage that nevertheless seems to contain genuine twisted affection. She is ugly, sharp-tongued, and dangerous. Their constant bickering provides some of the series' most memorable comedic moments.

korbolo dom.name: Korbolo Dom
korbolo dom.description: A renegade Malazan Fist who betrays the Empire and joins the Whirlwind rebellion in Seven Cities. He is directly responsible for the massacre of Coltaine's refugees at the gates of Aren, ordering the garrison to stand down while thousands were slaughtered. He is ambitious, cruel, and without honour. He later seizes power within the rebellion and commands the Army of the Apocalypse. He is eventually captured and executed for his treachery.

kamist reloe.name: Kamist Reloe
kamist reloe.description: A mage allied with Korbolo Dom during the Seven Cities rebellion. He provides sorcerous support for the renegade forces and is instrumental in several battles against Coltaine's army. He is ambitious and power-hungry, ultimately destroyed by forces beyond his control.

sha'ik.name: Sha'ik
sha'ik.description: The title of the prophetess of the Whirlwind rebellion in Seven Cities. The first Sha'ik is killed and the title passes to Felisin Paran, who becomes Sha'ik Reborn. The Sha'ik is the spiritual leader of the rebellion against Malazan rule, drawing power from the Whirlwind goddess. The role consumes both women who bear it.

leoman of the flails.name: Leoman of the Flails
leoman of the flails.description: A desert warrior and commander in the Whirlwind rebellion, one of Sha'ik's most capable military leaders. He wields twin flails in combat and is a shrewd tactician. After Sha'ik's death he retreats to Y'Ghatan where he orchestrates a devastating trap, burning the city rather than surrendering it. He is pragmatic, cynical about the rebellion's religious aspects, and ultimately disappears to pursue his own path.

karsa orlong.name: Karsa Orlong
karsa orlong.description: A giant Teblor warrior from the isolated mountain tribes who begins the series as an arrogant, violent raider with a simplistic worldview. His early actions include raiding lowland settlements and committing terrible acts. He is captured, enslaved, and through years of suffering and exposure to wider civilisation, undergoes a profound transformation. He retains his immense physical prowess and warrior pride but develops a philosophical opposition to civilisation itself, which he sees as a system that enslaves and diminishes people. He swears to shatter the chains of all civilisation. He kills a Forkrul Assail, defeats a Deragoth, and performs numerous other extraordinary feats. He stands well over seven feet tall and wields a stone sword made of flint. By the series' end he has become one of the most powerful mortals alive. His character arc from brutal barbarian to revolutionary philosopher is one of the most compelling in the series.

bidithal.name: Bidithal
bidithal.description: A dark priest within Sha'ik's camp in Raraku, a worshipper of shadow with deeply sinister and abusive practices. He is a manipulative and evil figure who preys on children. He is eventually killed by Karsa Orlong, one of the most satisfying deaths in the series.

l'oric.name: L'oric
l'oric.description: A High Mage operating within Sha'ik's army, secretly the son of Osserc (Osseric). He is thoughtful and observant, working his own agenda while navigating the dangerous politics of the Whirlwind camp. He plays an important role in events in Raraku and later in the broader conflict.

febryl.name: Febryl
febryl.description: A scheming mage in the Whirlwind rebellion who plots against Sha'ik. He is treacherous and self-serving, constantly manoeuvring for personal advantage within the dangerous politics of the rebel camp. His schemes ultimately fail and he meets a deserved end.

corabb bhilan thenu'alas.name: Corabb Bhilan Thenu'alas
corabb bhilan thenu'alas.description: A desert warrior who initially follows Leoman of the Flails in the Whirlwind rebellion. After Y'Ghatan he joins the Bonehunters and becomes a Malazan marine. He is absurdly, impossibly lucky — surviving situations that should kill him through sheer improbable fortune. He is earnest, devoted, and somewhat dim, which makes his incredible luck all the more entertaining. He becomes fiercely loyal to his new comrades.

trull sengar.name: Trull Sengar
trull sengar.description: A Tiste Edur warrior and the most thoughtful of the four Sengar brothers. He alone sees the corruption spreading through his people as his brother Rhulad falls under the Crippled God's influence. He opposes the invasion of Lether and speaks out against the madness consuming the Edur, for which he is Shorn — ritually cast out from his people by having all memory of him cut away. He is left chained between two realms as punishment. He is freed and eventually finds purpose alongside the T'lan Imass Onrack, with whom he forms one of the series' most moving friendships. He falls in love with Seren Pedac. He is killed by the assassin Sirryn Kanar in a sudden, devastating moment that is deliberately anticlimactic to emphasize the randomness of death in war. He fights with a spear and is one of the finest warriors among the Edur.

rhulad sengar.name: Rhulad Sengar
rhulad sengar.description: The youngest Sengar brother, initially insecure and envious of his brothers' accomplishments. He discovers a cursed sword on a battlefield, a weapon forged by the Crippled God, and upon touching it is killed and resurrected with coins fused to his skin. Each time he is killed in battle he returns to life in screaming agony, each death making him more powerful but also more insane. He becomes Emperor of the Tiste Edur and then of Lether, but his reign is a tortured nightmare as the Crippled God whispers through the sword. He becomes a tragic, pitiable figure — a young man destroyed by power he never sought. He can only find peace when Karsa Orlong kills him with a weapon that the cursed sword cannot overcome, and Rhulad finally chooses not to return.

fear sengar.name: Fear Sengar
fear sengar.description: The eldest Sengar brother, Weapons Master of the Tiste Edur. He is honourable, dutiful, and deeply troubled by what happens to Rhulad. He sets out on a quest to find Scabandari's soul and a way to free his brother from the cursed sword. He is a formidable warrior and a natural leader, representing the best of what the Edur could be before their corruption.

binadas sengar.name: Binadas Sengar
binadas sengar.description: One of the Sengar brothers, a skilled mage among the Tiste Edur. He is quieter than his brothers and more attuned to the natural and magical world. He is killed during the events surrounding the Edur invasion, a loss that further fractures the family.

seren pedac.name: Seren Pedac
seren pedac.description: A Letherii Acquitor (trade diplomat) who becomes entangled with the Tiste Edur invasion of Lether. She initially serves as a liaison between the two cultures but is swept up in the war. She is traumatised by violence done to her and struggles to recover. She falls in love with Trull Sengar and carries his child. She is a complex, wounded character whose journey reflects the personal cost of political upheaval.

hull beddict.name: Hull Beddict
hull beddict.description: The eldest Beddict brother, a man consumed by guilt over his role in Letherii atrocities against subject peoples. He once served the kingdom faithfully but became disillusioned when he witnessed the destruction of tribes he had been sent to pacify. He defects to the Tiste Edur cause, believing they will bring justice, only to discover they are no better. His tragedy is that of a man who cannot find a side worth fighting for.

tehol beddict.name: Tehol Beddict
tehol beddict.description: A genius economist of Letheras, eldest surviving Beddict brother, who appears to be a lazy eccentric living on his roof with his manservant Bugg. In reality he is orchestrating the complete economic collapse of the Letherii Empire as revenge for its cruelties. He understands the debt-based Letherii economic system so thoroughly that he can manipulate it from poverty. He is witty, irreverent, and engages in constant comedic banter with Bugg. Despite his humorous exterior he is driven by genuine moral outrage at how Letherii society destroys subject peoples for profit. He eventually becomes King of Lether after the old order falls. His relationship with Bugg (who is actually the Elder God Mael) is one of the great comedic and emotional partnerships in the series.

bugg.name: Bugg
bugg.description: Tehol Beddict's manservant, a humble-seeming old man who is actually Mael, the Elder God of the Seas, in disguise. He serves Tehol out of genuine affection and shared purpose. He is immensely powerful but chooses to live simply, performing domestic chores and building terrible walls. He only reveals his power when absolutely necessary, and when he does the results are devastating. The contrast between his mundane exterior and cosmic nature is a source of both comedy and awe.

brys beddict.name: Brys Beddict
brys beddict.description: The youngest Beddict brother and the King's Champion of Lether, considered the finest swordsman in the kingdom. He is dutiful, honourable, and devoted to his king even as the Letherii system grows increasingly corrupt. He fights Rhulad Sengar in single combat and nearly wins through pure skill before being killed by sorcery. He is later resurrected and serves under the new order, eventually commanding Letherii forces allied with the Bonehunters. He is the most conventionally heroic of the Beddict brothers.

shurq elalle.name: Shurq Elalle
shurq elalle.description: An undead thief of Letheras with a dry, morbid sense of humour. She was killed and then raised from death but continues to function, struggling with the physical realities of being a walking corpse. She is pragmatic, self-serving, and surprisingly likeable. She becomes involved in Tehol's schemes and later captains a ship.

ublala pung.name: Ublala Pung
ublala pung.description: A dim but immensely strong Tarthenal half-blood in Letheras. He is sweet-natured, easily confused, and blessed with extraordinary physical endowments that various women find appealing. He is used as muscle by various factions but is too simple to understand the schemes around him. He provides comic relief but also has moments of genuine courage.

kettle.name: Kettle
kettle.description: An undead child who guards the Azath tower in Letheras. She was killed and reanimated by the dying Azath House to serve as its protector. She is eerie, matter-of-fact about her condition, and devoted to her duty. She eventually plays a role in the resurrection of Silchas Ruin from his Azath prison.

silchas ruin.name: Silchas Ruin
silchas ruin.description: Anomander Rake's brother, known as the White Crow. He is a Tiste Andii Soletaken Eleint with white skin, an anomaly among his people. He was betrayed by the Tiste Edur Scabandari Bloodeye and imprisoned within an Azath House for hundreds of thousands of years. Upon his release he is single-minded and ruthless, seeking power and vengeance. He is feared by nearly everyone he encounters. Unlike Rake's compassionate nature, Silchas is colder and more pragmatic, though he ultimately serves a role in the larger scheme of things. He dual-wields swords and is a devastatingly effective fighter in both humanoid and dragon forms.

udinaas.name: Udinaas
udinaas.description: A Letherii slave in the Sengar household who is bitten by a Wyval (a draconic parasite) and becomes bound to Menandore's blood. He is intelligent, bitter about his enslavement, and possesses a sharp tongue that he cannot control even when it endangers him. He becomes a reluctant participant in vast events and eventually becomes the father of a child of great significance. He is one of the most clear-eyed observers of the Edur-Letherii conflict.

onrack.name: Onrack
onrack.description: A T'lan Imass warrior who regains mortality through the magic of the Eres'al. He forms a profound friendship with Trull Sengar, two outcasts from their respective peoples who find brotherhood with each other. As a mortal Imass he rediscovers emotion, love, and the beauty of the living world. He is a talented artist who carved images even as an undead warrior. His restoration to life and reunion with his ancient love Kilava is one of the series' most beautiful storylines.

beak.name: Beak
beak.description: A young Bonehunter mage of extraordinary latent power who was horrifically abused as a child by his family. He is gentle, innocent, and sees the world in terms of coloured candles representing the warrens. He is terrified of revealing the full extent of his abilities. During a desperate moment he opens every warren he possesses simultaneously to create a protective shell over an entire army, saving thousands of soldiers at the cost of his own life. His sacrifice is made all the more devastating by his childlike nature and the fact that he wanted only to make his sergeant proud. He is mourned by the entire army and his death is one of the most emotional in the series.

faradan sort.name: Faradan Sort
faradan sort.description: A Bonehunter officer, a strict and capable captain who runs her company with iron discipline. She is tough, competent, and cares for her soldiers beneath her hard exterior. She recognises Beak's potential and treats him with a kindness that means the world to him.

bottle.name: Bottle
bottle.description: A Bonehunter squad mage with spirit-walking abilities inherited from his grandmother, a Spiritwalker of the Kanese tradition. He can send his consciousness into animals and use them as spies. He is clever, resourceful, and often serves as the eyes and ears of the Bonehunter command through his animal scouts. He is drawn into increasingly dangerous sorcerous situations but survives through wit rather than raw power.

smiles.name: Smiles
smiles.description: A Bonehunter soldier from Dal Hon with a vicious temperament and skill with throwing knives. She is mean-spirited, competitive, and constantly needling her squadmates, but she is a dependable fighter when it matters. Her hostile exterior masks deeper vulnerabilities.

koryk.name: Koryk
koryk.description: A Bonehunter half-blood Seti soldier, skilled fighter with a complicated relationship to his mixed heritage. He is tough, capable, and struggles with identity issues stemming from belonging fully to neither of his parent cultures.

tarr.name: Tarr
tarr.description: A Bonehunter corporal, the embodiment of solid, dependable heavy infantry. He is not flashy or brilliant but utterly reliable in a fight. He carries a shield and holds the line when everything else falls apart.

cuttle.name: Cuttle
cuttle.description: A Bonehunter veteran sapper who serves alongside Fiddler. He is experienced, professional, and carries a personal arsenal of Moranth munitions. He is one of the steady hands in the squad, a counterpoint to younger, less experienced soldiers.

gesler.name: Gesler
gesler.description: A Bonehunter sergeant who, along with Stormy, was transformed by exposure to the fires of Telas warren. The transformation gave them both golden skin, enhanced strength, and a connection to fire magic. He is a natural leader, calm under pressure, and deeply bonded with his partner Stormy. He eventually becomes the Mortal Sword of the K'Chain Che'Malle, leading their armies in the final war. He dies in the last battle.

stormy.name: Stormy
stormy.description: A Bonehunter marine, Gesler's lifelong companion, equally transformed by Telas fire. He is big, red-haired, loud, and perpetually annoyed. He becomes the Shield Anvil of the K'Chain Che'Malle despite wanting nothing to do with the role. He and Gesler share a bond that transcends ordinary friendship, built over decades of shared combat. He dies alongside Gesler in the final battle.

truth.name: Truth
truth.description: A young soldier in Gesler's squad, earnest and brave. He represents the innocent idealism of youth in contrast to the weary veterans around him. He proves his courage in battle and earns the respect of his more experienced comrades.

deadsmell.name: Deadsmell
deadsmell.description: A Bonehunter corporal with necromantic abilities. He can communicate with and raise the dead, a talent that is useful but deeply unsettling to those around him. He is pragmatic about his abilities and uses them in service to his squad.

throatslitter.name: Throatslitter
throatslitter.description: A Bonehunter soldier from a criminal background, named for his preferred method of killing. He is lethal, quiet, and effective. Despite his grim name he is a reliable squad member who fights with deadly efficiency.

widdershins.name: Widdershins
widdershins.description: A Bonehunter squad mage, one of the many magic users scattered through Tavore's army. He provides magical support to his squad and navigates the increasingly dangerous sorcerous landscape of the series' later books.

skulldeath.name: Skulldeath
skulldeath.description: A Bonehunter warrior of exceptional swordsmanship, quiet and deadly. He is young, beautiful, and fights with an almost supernatural grace. He barely speaks but his blade speaks volumes. He becomes Hellian's unlikely romantic interest.

hellian.name: Hellian
hellian.description: A Bonehunter sergeant who is perpetually drunk yet surprisingly effective in command. She has a paralysing fear of spiders and uses alcohol to cope with her anxieties. Despite being barely functional between battles, she transforms in combat into a capable and even inspiring leader. Her drunken antics provide considerable comic relief.

urb.name: Urb
urb.description: A Bonehunter sergeant, a large, quiet man who is secretly in love with Hellian. He is steady, dependable, and utterly devoted to her despite her obliviousness. He is a solid fighter and reliable leader who provides stability to the soldiers around him.

nefarias bredd.name: Nefarias Bredd
nefarias bredd.description: A Bonehunter soldier of near-mythical reputation who is credited with extraordinary feats of solo combat. He may or may not actually exist — his exploits are so outlandish that he may be a collective fiction created by bored soldiers. His ambiguous reality is a running joke throughout the Bonehunter books.

banaschar.name: Banaschar
banaschar.description: A former high priest of D'rek, the Worm of Autumn, who has fallen into alcoholic despair after the goddess destroyed her own priesthood. He joins the Bonehunters as a dissolute hanger-on but carries knowledge and power that prove crucial. He represents religious crisis and the loss of faith, drowning his betrayal by his goddess in drink.

nimander golit.name: Nimander Golit
nimander golit.description: A young Tiste Andii, son of Anomander Rake, who leads a group of Andii youths on a journey that mirrors his father's own struggles with leadership and purpose. He is uncertain, self-doubting, and burdened by the weight of being Rake's son without Rake's certainty. His journey is one of coming into his own as a leader.

phaed.name: Phaed
phaed.description: One of Nimander's Tiste Andii companions, manipulative, cruel, and possibly sociopathic. She is a poisonous presence within the group, deliberately undermining others and causing harm. She represents the worst aspects of the Andii's loss of purpose.

desra.name: Desra
desra.description: A Tiste Andii woman among Nimander's group. She is passionate and fierce, one of the more emotionally volatile members of the young Andii. She has a physical relationship with Clip.

nenanda.name: Nenanda
nenanda.description: A warrior among Nimander's Tiste Andii, proud and eager to prove himself through combat. He is skilled with a blade and impatient with the group's indecision, wanting to act rather than talk.

skintick.name: Skintick
skintick.description: A witty and philosophical member of Nimander's Tiste Andii group. He uses humour to cope with the darkness of their situation and serves as the group's emotional observer. He is perceptive and articulate, often the voice of reason amid chaos.

aranatha.name: Aranatha
aranatha.description: A seemingly gentle Tiste Andii woman in Nimander's group who harbours a hidden nature. She appears passive and distant but is actually carrying something far greater within her. Her true nature is revealed at a critical moment.

kedeviss.name: Kedeviss
kedeviss.description: A capable fighter among Nimander's Tiste Andii companions. She is practical, level-headed, and one of the more grounded members of the group. She has strong feelings for Nimander.

clip.name: Clip
clip.description: A Tiste Andii from Black Coral who guides Nimander's group, constantly spinning a chain with two rings on it. He is arrogant, dismissive, and contemptuous of Nimander's leadership. He is eventually revealed to have been corrupted by a dark force that uses him as a vessel.

kallor.name: Kallor
kallor.description: The High King, an ancient warlord cursed over a hundred thousand years ago by three Elder Gods after he destroyed an entire continent and its population rather than surrender power. His curses are threefold: he will never ascend to godhood despite his power, he will never achieve lasting rule, and he will age without dying. He is bitter, arrogant, and constantly seeking to overthrow the curses by seizing power. He allies with Caladan Brood against the Malazan Empire but betrays the alliance. He kills Whiskeyjack and attempts to kill Silverfox. Despite his villainy he is a complex figure whose enormous lifespan has given him a terrible understanding of the futility of mortal ambition. He is still dangerous in the final books and remains a threat throughout.

caladan brood.name: Caladan Brood
caladan brood.description: A mysterious warlord who leads the alliance against the Malazan Empire in Genabackis alongside Anomander Rake. He wields a massive hammer that has the power to wake the sleeping goddess Burn, which would essentially end the world. This gives him a doomsday deterrent that makes even gods cautious around him. He is patient, honourable, and slow to anger but devastating when roused. He is possibly of Thelomen (giant) descent. He reluctantly allies with the Malazan forces against the Pannion Domin and his fury at Silverfox's refusal to release the T'lan Imass nearly causes him to use the hammer.

korlat.name: Korlat
korlat.description: A Tiste Andii Soletaken, second in command to Anomander Rake in Moon's Spawn. She is a capable warrior and dragon who becomes Whiskeyjack's lover during the Pannion War campaign. His death devastates her and she carries that grief for the remainder of the series. She represents the possibility of love between Andii and human, and the pain that comes from such connections given their vastly different lifespans.

orfantal.name: Orfantal
orfantal.description: A young Tiste Andii from the time of Kharkanas, appearing in the prequel novels. He is a child during the events of the Kharkanas trilogy and his innocence provides contrast to the darkness consuming Andii society.

spinnock durav.name: Spinnock Durav
spinnock durav.description: A Tiste Andii warrior loyal to Anomander Rake, tasked with guarding Black Coral. He is one of Rake's most devoted followers, a skilled fighter who spends his time playing board games with the locals. He is given the task of delaying Kallor during a critical moment and does so through an extended game of Kef Tanar, demonstrating extraordinary courage.

endest silann.name: Endest Silann
endest silann.description: An elderly Tiste Andii mage, one of Rake's oldest followers. He carries deep guilt from the ancient events of Kharkanas and the role he played in them. He is weary, haunted, and represents the toll that millennia of existence takes on the Andii soul. Despite his apparent frailty he commands considerable sorcerous power.

itkovian.name: Itkovian
itkovian.description: The Shield Anvil of the Grey Swords mercenary company in Capustan. He is a deeply compassionate man whose religious role requires him to absorb the grief and suffering of others into himself. During the siege of Capustan by the Pannion Domin's forces, he takes in the suffering of the entire city and then, in an act of extraordinary compassion, takes in the grief of the T'lan Imass, hundreds of thousands of years of accumulated sorrow from their undeath. This act is so immense that it effectively makes him an Ascendant of compassion. He dies from the sheer weight of what he has absorbed, but his sacrifice is considered one of the most profound acts of mercy in the entire series. He is quiet, dutiful, and utterly selfless.

gruntle.name: Gruntle
gruntle.description: A caravan guard who reluctantly becomes the Mortal Sword of Treach, the Tiger of Summer, during the siege of Capustan. He is a big, scarred, pragmatic man who wants nothing to do with gods or destiny. The power of Treach transforms him physically, giving him tiger-like features and supernatural fighting ability. He becomes one of the most feared warriors on the continent despite his constant desire to just be left alone. He is devoted to his companion Stonny Menackis and is deeply affected by the violence done to her. His resistance to his divine role and eventual grudging acceptance of it provides an earthy counterpoint to more willing champions.

stonny menackis.name: Stonny Menackis
stonny menackis.description: A tough caravan guard and companion of Gruntle. She is sharp-tongued, independent, and a skilled fighter. She suffers terribly during the siege of Capustan and the aftermath profoundly changes her. She later raises a child and her story becomes intertwined with the fate of Darujhistan.

harllo.name: Harllo
harllo.description: A name shared by two characters: Stonny's lost child, and a boy in Darujhistan who works in the mines and has a hidden lineage connected to Darujhistan's nobility. The child Harllo's story in the later books is one of resilience and the search for belonging.

bauchelain.name: Bauchelain
bauchelain.description: A necromancer of vast and amoral intellect, one half of a wandering duo with Korbal Broach. He is urbane, charming in a terrifying way, and utterly without moral restraint in his pursuit of knowledge. He treats atrocities with academic detachment. He and Korbal wander the world leaving destruction in their wake, accompanied by their long-suffering manservant Emancipor Reese. They are the protagonists of their own novella series.

korbal broach.name: Korbal Broach
korbal broach.description: An insane surgeon-mage and partner of Bauchelain. He collects body parts, creates horrific undead constructs, and is genuinely psychotic in contrast to Bauchelain's cold intellectualism. He is round, childlike in some ways, and utterly terrifying. His experiments are the stuff of nightmares.

emancipor reese.name: Emancipor Reese
emancipor reese.description: The long-suffering manservant to Bauchelain and Korbal Broach. He is a hapless, hen-pecked man whose wives keep dying under suspicious circumstances. He serves his monstrous employers out of a combination of terror and the fact that the pay is good. He provides the everyman perspective on the horrors his employers perpetrate.

picker.name: Picker
picker.description: A Bridgeburner corporal who survives the company's destruction and opens a tavern in Darujhistan with Blend. She is practical, tough, and no-nonsense. She leads the surviving Bridgeburners in their civilian lives and is drawn back into danger when threats emerge in Darujhistan.

blend.name: Blend
blend.description: A Bridgeburner soldier with a supernatural talent for being unnoticed — people's eyes simply slide past her. She is Picker's partner in both business and life. She is quiet, observant, and utterly lethal when necessary.

antsy.name: Antsy
antsy.description: A nervous, paranoid Bridgeburner sergeant who survives to retire in Darujhistan. He is constantly worried about conspiracies, poisons, and threats both real and imagined. His paranoia is often played for comedy but occasionally proves justified.

bluepearl.name: Bluepearl
bluepearl.description: A Bridgeburner mage of Napan heritage (characterised by blue-tinted skin). He survives to retirement and works at the tavern with the other surviving Bridgeburners. He provides magical support when the group is drawn back into conflict.

mallet.name: Mallet
mallet.description: The Bridgeburner healer, a Denul practitioner who is one of the finest military healers in the Malazan forces. He is gentle, dedicated, and deeply valued by his comrades. He represents the compassionate side of the military, constantly fighting to save lives in a world bent on destroying them.

trotts.name: Trotts
trotts.description: A Bridgeburner warrior of Barghast blood, large and fierce. He serves with distinction and represents the Barghast presence within the Malazan military. He is a formidable fighter who takes pride in his Barghast heritage.

hedge.name: Hedge
hedge.description: A Bridgeburner sapper and Fiddler's best friend. He is killed during the tunnelling beneath Capustan but returns as a ghost, existing between life and death. He eventually finds his way back to a form of life and rejoins the Bonehunters, bringing his sapper expertise and irrepressible spirit back to the squad. He and Fiddler share a bond that transcends death itself. He is cheerful, reckless with explosives, and utterly fearless. His reunion with Fiddler is one of the most emotionally satisfying moments in the series.

noto boil.name: Noto Boil
noto boil.description: A Bonehunter company cutter (medic) who is largely incompetent at his job. He rides a cow instead of a horse and provides dark comic relief. Despite his lack of medical skill he survives through the campaigns and becomes an unlikely fixture of the Bonehunter army.

silverfox.name: Silverfox
silverfox.description: A being born from the convergence of three souls: Tattersail, Nightchill, and Bellurdan. She is carried and born by a Rhivi woman and grows at an accelerated rate. She is a Bonecaster of immense power with the ability to command the T'lan Imass and potentially release them from their undeath. Her refusal to do so during the Pannion War, choosing instead to gather the Imass for another purpose, causes a rift with Caladan Brood and others. She is a complex figure torn between the needs of multiple souls within her and the expectations placed upon her by others.

nightchill.name: Nightchill
nightchill.description: A Malazan mage of extraordinary power, actually an Elder Goddess in disguise, who is killed at the Siege of Pale by Tayschrenn's betrayal. Her soul is reborn within Silverfox, contributing divine power to the combined being. She is one of the three souls that make Silverfox so powerful and conflicted.

bellurdan.name: Bellurdan
bellurdan.description: A Thelomen giant and mage, killed at Pale alongside Nightchill. His soul merges into Silverfox. He was deeply devoted to Nightchill and his presence within Silverfox contributes a gentle, loyal nature.

pran chole.name: Pran Chole
pran chole.description: An ancient Bonecaster of the Kron T'lan Imass who participates in the earliest events of the series, including the ritual at the barrow of the Jaghut Tyrant. He represents the original Imass and their ancient war against the Jaghut.

kilava.name: Kilava
kilava.description: An Imass Bonecaster who refused the Ritual of Tellann, choosing to remain mortal and alive. She has survived for hundreds of thousands of years through her own power, becoming a Soletaken. She is the sister of Onos T'oolan and eventually reunites with Onrack. She is wild, fierce, and represents what the Imass could have been had they not chosen undeath.

onos t'oolan.name: Onos T'oolan
onos t'oolan.description: Tool's full name as a T'lan Imass. He is one of the most ancient and skilled warriors of the Imass, who served the Malazan Empire before being freed and eventually restored to mortality. See Tool entry above for full description.

logros.name: Logros
logros.description: Commander of the Logros T'lan Imass, the clan that served the Malazan Empire. He is a T'lan Imass leader who directed his people in service to Kellanved and later Laseen. His clan becomes fragmented and scattered over the course of the series.

ibra gholan.name: Ibra Gholan
ibra gholan.description: A T'lan Imass warrior of the Logros clan, one of the undead warriors who serves in various capacities throughout the series. He is a skilled fighter and tracker.

lanas tog.name: Lanas Tog
lanas tog.description: A T'lan Imass sent as a messenger to find other Imass armies scattered across the world. Her journey takes her to distant lands and her reports drive several plot developments.

olar ethil.name: Olar Ethil
olar ethil.description: The first Bonecaster, an extremely ancient and powerful Imass spirit who is one of the original architects of the Ritual of Tellann. She is manipulative, seemingly mad, and driven by agendas that span hundreds of thousands of years. She is a Soletaken who can take the form of a giant skeletal beast. She is one of the most ambiguous figures in the series — sometimes appearing to help, sometimes appearing to hinder, always serving her own inscrutable purposes.

ulshun pral.name: Ulshun Pral
ulshun pral.description: A living (pre-Ritual) Imass encountered by Onrack in a pocket realm that exists outside normal time. His existence proves that not all Imass underwent the Ritual, and his community represents a preserved fragment of what the Imass once were as a living people.

torrent.name: Torrent
torrent.description: An Awl warrior, the last of his people after their destruction. He is reluctant, bitter, and tasked with protecting children despite wanting only to die with his people. He gradually finds purpose in his guardian role and grows beyond his initial despair.

setoc.name: Setoc
setoc.description: A young woman raised by wolves who becomes the Destriant of the Wolves (Togg and Fanderay). She is wild, half-feral, and struggles to bridge the gap between her wolf nature and her human form. She serves as a bridge between the beast gods and the mortal world.

gall.name: Gall
gall.description: Warleader of the Khundryl Burned Tears who swears his mounted warriors to Adjunct Tavore's cause. He is a proud, capable horse-warrior leader who struggles with the losses his people suffer in service to a cause they don't fully understand. His honour binds him to his oath even as the cost mounts.

hanavat.name: Hanavat
hanavat.description: Gall's wife, a strong Khundryl matriarch who manages the clan's affairs while her husband leads in war. She is tough, practical, and provides emotional grounding for the Khundryl storyline. She gives birth during the campaign, symbolising life continuing amid death.

badalle.name: Badalle
badalle.description: A child poet among the Snake, a column of refugee children crossing the Glass Desert toward Kolanse. She composes poetry that captures the horror and resilience of the children's march. She is preternaturally articulate and her words carry a power that borders on the sorcerous. Her poetry is some of the most haunting writing in the series.

saddic.name: Saddic
saddic.description: A child companion of Badalle in the Snake, one of the surviving refugee children who crosses the Glass Desert. He is devoted to Badalle and helps her lead the other children.

rutt.name: Rutt
rutt.description: A boy who leads the Snake, the column of refugee children, carrying a baby named Held on his back throughout the entire march. He barely speaks but his silent determination to keep walking and to protect the baby makes him one of the most affecting figures in the series.

brayderal.name: Brayderal
brayderal.description: A Forkrul Assail disguised among the Perish Grey Helms. She infiltrates the religious military order and works to corrupt it from within, eventually succeeding in turning a portion of the Perish against the Bonehunters.

grub.name: Grub
grub.description: A mysterious child found at the Chain of Dogs, possibly connected to Coltaine's death. He grows up among the Bonehunters and demonstrates odd knowledge and abilities that suggest he is more than he appears. He is connected to the land and its spirits in ways that others cannot understand.

sinn.name: Sinn
sinn.description: A young Malazan mage of terrifying power, a child who commands fire magic of extraordinary destructive force. She barely speaks and her mental state is unstable. She and Grub have a complicated, unsettling connection. Her power makes her one of the most dangerous assets in the Bonehunter army, but her instability makes her equally threatening to her own side.

apt.name: Apt
apt.description: A demon of Shadow, a large, winged creature who adopts and protects a young orphan boy named Panek. She is maternal, fierce, and loyal. She serves Shadowthrone's interests while genuinely caring for her adopted child, providing one of the series' more unusual parent-child relationships.

panek.name: Panek
panek.description: An orphaned boy adopted by the Shadow demon Apt. He grows up in the Shadow Realm and is raised among the other children protected there. He serves as evidence that even demons can love.

cotillion.name: Cotillion
cotillion.description: The god of assassins and Patron of High House Shadow, formerly the mortal man known as Dancer, who was partner to Emperor Kellanved. He and Kellanved faked their deaths and ascended to godhood, becoming Cotillion and Shadowthrone. Unlike his eccentric partner, Cotillion is thoughtful, empathetic, and increasingly conflicted about the manipulations their plans require. He possessed the girl Apsalar to use as an assassin and carries lasting guilt for it. Over the course of the series he emerges as one of the most genuinely compassionate figures among the gods, working toward the Crippled God's freedom while understanding the personal cost. He is tall, lean, and prefers rope weapons. His relationship with Shadowthrone is one of mutual respect masked by constant bickering.

shadowthrone.name: Shadowthrone
shadowthrone.description: The King of High House Shadow, formerly Emperor Kellanved of the Malazan Empire. He is eccentric, devious, prone to maniacal laughter, and seemingly insane. He is also one of the greatest strategic minds in existence, orchestrating plans that span decades. He appears as a small, hunched figure in dark robes, wielding a cane. His apparent madness conceals the fact that virtually everything he does serves a purpose in an enormously complex scheme to free the Crippled God and rebalance the world. He has an alliance of convenience and genuine friendship with Cotillion, whom he trusts absolutely even while mocking him constantly.

ammanas.name: Ammanas
ammanas.description: Another name for Shadowthrone, used primarily in older texts and by those who knew him before ascension. See Shadowthrone.

kellanved.name: Kellanved
kellanved.description: The founder and first Emperor of the Malazan Empire, a Napan (or possibly Dal Honese) mage of extraordinary intelligence and ambition. He built the Empire from nothing alongside his partner Dancer, conquering vast territories through cunning, magic, and military innovation. He was apparently assassinated by Surly (Laseen) but had in fact arranged his own ascension to godhood, becoming Shadowthrone. His mortal reign is remembered as both brilliant and brutal.

dancer.name: Dancer
dancer.description: Kellanved's companion, the greatest assassin in the Malazan Empire, who ascended to become the god Cotillion. See Cotillion for full description.

empress laseen.name: Empress Laseen
empress laseen.description: Formerly Surly, commander of the Claw, who seized the Malazan throne by arranging the apparent assassination of Kellanved and Dancer. She rules the Empire with an iron hand but faces constant threats from rebellion, internal politics, and the machinations of the very gods her predecessors became. She is pragmatic, ruthless, and isolated. Her purge of the Old Guard military leaders (including the Bridgeburners) is driven by paranoia and political necessity. She is ultimately a tragic figure — a capable administrator who took power through murder and spent her entire reign paying for it.

topper.name: Topper
topper.description: The Clawmaster of the Malazan Empire under Laseen, head of the imperial intelligence and assassination service. He is arrogant, capable, and loyal to the Empress. He operates in the shadows, directing Claw operations across the Empire.

possum.name: Possum
possum.description: A Claw operative under Laseen, one of the agents who carries out the Empress's darker orders. He operates in the murky world of imperial espionage and assassination.

pearl.name: Pearl
pearl.description: A Claw agent tasked with various missions in Seven Cities. He is suave, skilled, and develops a complicated relationship with Lostara Yil during their shared missions. He is charming and deadly in equal measure but meets his end in the complex web of imperial politics.

lostara yil.name: Lostara Yil
lostara yil.description: A former Red Blade (imperial loyalist militia in Seven Cities) who becomes Pearl's partner during operations in Seven Cities and later serves as adjunct and bodyguard to Tavore. She is a dancer by training, which gives her extraordinary grace in combat. She is loyal, fierce, and one of the few people who remains consistently at Tavore's side.

ceda kuru qan.name: Ceda Kuru Qan
ceda kuru qan.description: The chief mage of the Letherii kingdom, an ancient and immensely powerful sorcerer who draws on the Hold of Tiles for his magic. He is elderly, seemingly frail, but commands forces that can challenge gods. He serves the king faithfully and is one of the last defenders of the old Letherii order against the Edur invasion. He sacrifices himself in a titanic magical battle.

hannan mosag.name: Hannan Mosag
hannan mosag.description: The Warlock King of the Tiste Edur, the most powerful sorcerer among the shadow-skinned people. He makes a secret pact with the Crippled God in exchange for power, which ultimately leads to the corruption and fall of the Edur. He is ambitious, proud, and ultimately a puppet of forces beyond his control. He is physically broken over the course of the series, reduced to a crawling wreck, but retains his magical potency.

mayen.name: Mayen
mayen.description: An Edur woman betrothed to Fear Sengar but taken by Rhulad after his transformation. She is a victim of the power dynamics within the Sengar household and the wider Edur corruption. Her suffering is one of many personal tragedies caused by the Crippled God's influence.

janath.name: Janath
janath.description: A Letherii scholar and academic imprisoned for seditious teachings against the state. She is intellectually brilliant and outspoken, qualities that make her dangerous to the ruling order. She is rescued and becomes Tehol's lover. Her character represents the suppression of truth by corrupt systems.

turudal brizad.name: Turudal Brizad
turudal brizad.description: A mysterious figure in the Letherii court, actually the Errant (Errastas), an Elder God, in disguise. He manipulates events from within the court, pushing and pulling fate according to his own designs. His true nature is a closely guarded secret.

gerun eberict.name: Gerun Eberict
gerun eberict.description: A Letherii Liberty Consign enforcer, a brutal and efficient killer employed by the mercantile class to enforce debts and eliminate threats. He is sociopathic, powerful, and feared throughout Letheras. He is eventually killed by Brys Beddict.

brohl handar.name: Brohl Handar
brohl handar.description: A Tiste Edur Overseer assigned to govern a Letherii region after the conquest. He is honourable by Edur standards and struggles with the moral complexities of occupation. He gradually comes to understand the cost of the Edur conquest and his own role in it.

yan tovis (twilight).name: Yan Tovis (Twilight)
yan tovis (twilight).description: An Atri-Preda of mixed Shake and Letherii blood who discovers her heritage as the Queen of the Shake, an ancient coastal people. She is torn between her Letherii military career and her Shake destiny. She eventually leads her people on a desperate march to the Shore, the boundary between Light and Darkness, where they must make their stand. She is practical, burdened by responsibility, and forced to make impossible choices.

yedan derryg (the watch).name: Yedan Derryg (The Watch)
yedan derryg (the watch).description: Half-brother of Twilight, a legendary warrior who takes on the role of defending the Shore against the Tiste Liosan. He is possibly the greatest swordsman alive during the events of the final books. He fights an essentially endless battle against waves of Liosan invaders, holding the breach through superhuman endurance and skill. His defence of the Shore is one of the most epic martial feats in the series.

sandalath drukorlat.name: Sandalath Drukorlat
sandalath drukorlat.description: A Tiste Andii woman from the ancient age of Kharkanas who survived into the present era. She is married to Withal the Meckros swordsmith. She carries the memories and trauma of the original sundering of the Tiste and struggles with her place in a world that has changed beyond recognition.

withal.name: Withal
withal.description: A Meckros swordsmith who was enslaved by the Crippled God and forced to forge the cursed sword that later binds Rhulad Sengar. He is freed and married to Sandalath Drukorlat. He is practical, skilled with his hands, and uncomfortable with the larger events swirling around him.

mael.name: Mael
mael.description: The Elder God of the Seas, one of the most powerful beings in existence. He disguises himself as Bugg, Tehol Beddict's manservant, living a humble life in Letheras. He is ancient, weary of divine conflicts, and genuinely fond of Tehol. When he reveals his power it is devastating — he can summon tidal waves and command the oceans. He plays a crucial role in the fall of the Letherii empire and the broader cosmic events.

k'rul.name: K'rul
k'rul.description: An Elder God whose blood literally forms the warrens that mages use for sorcery. He sacrificed his own life-force to create the entire system of magic that underlies the world. He is ancient, generous, and has been mostly dormant for millennia, weakened by his sacrifice. He is reawakened by events in Darujhistan and continues to work quietly behind the scenes. He manifests in Darujhistan where his temple stands and works with Kruppe and others. He is one of the most fundamentally important beings in the cosmos, yet one of the most humble.

draconus.name: Draconus
draconus.description: An Elder God and the creator of the sword Dragnipur. He forged the sword to create a pocket dimension inside it that would keep Darkness ahead of the encroaching Chaos at the Gate of Darkness. He was himself slain by Anomander Rake using Dragnipur, trapping him inside his own creation where he spent millennia helping to drag the wagon that keeps Chaos at bay. He is eventually freed when Dragnipur is shattered. He is a figure of immense power and complexity — creator, lover of Mother Dark, father of Envy and Spite, and a being who made terrible choices for reasons he believed were necessary.

burn.name: Burn
burn.description: The Sleeping Goddess, the living earth itself. Her body is the physical world and she sleeps beneath it. If she is awakened the world would be destroyed and remade. She is being poisoned by the Crippled God's presence, which threatens to kill her in her sleep. Caladan Brood's hammer has the power to wake her, serving as a doomsday weapon.

hood.name: Hood
hood.description: The God of Death and King of High House Death. He was originally a Jaghut who declared war on Death itself after losing someone he loved, eventually conquering death's realm and becoming its ruler. He presides over the paths of the dead from his throne. He is ancient, sardonic, and weary. He eventually relinquishes his throne deliberately, stepping down from godhood in a pivotal act that reshuffles the cosmic order. His character reflects the Jaghut tendency toward dark humour and solitary determination.

fener.name: Fener
fener.description: The God of War, the Boar of Summer. He is one of the most widely worshipped gods among soldiers. He is pulled from his realm during the events of the series and forced into mortal form, which creates a crisis in the divine hierarchy. His fall from godhood is connected to Heboric's storyline. He is eventually replaced in his role.

togg.name: Togg
togg.description: The male Wolf God of War, the Wolf of Winter. He and Fanderay represent a more primal, bestial aspect of war than Fener. They are worshipped by the Perish Grey Helms and later by others. They represent the hunt, the pack, and the wild nature of conflict.

fanderay.name: Fanderay
fanderay.description: The female Wolf Goddess of War, the Wolf of Winter. She is Togg's mate and together they form the divine pairing that the Perish Grey Helms worship. She represents the feminine aspect of predatory warfare.

poliel.name: Poliel
poliel.description: The Goddess of Pestilence, Mistress of Plague. She is one of the darker deities, spreading disease and suffering. She is feared rather than worshipped by most and her interventions in mortal affairs are always devastating.

soliel.name: Soliel
soliel.description: The Goddess of Healing, counterpart to the destructive forces of other gods. She is associated with the Denul warren and represents the divine aspect of restoration and mercy.

beru.name: Beru
beru.description: A god associated with storms and sky, part of the larger divine pantheon. He is one of the less prominent deities but holds a place in the High Houses.

dessembrae.name: Dessembrae
dessembrae.description: The Lord of Tragedy, also known as the Lord of Tears. He is actually Dassem Ultor, the former First Sword of the Malazan Empire, who ascended after his apparent death. He embodies grief, loss, and the tragic nature of the mortal condition. See Dassem Ultor for his mortal history.

treach.name: Treach
treach.description: The God of War, the Tiger of Summer. He ascended from mortality, having been a Soletaken First Hero. He replaces Fener's aspect of war with a more feral, predatory divine force. His Mortal Sword is Gruntle.

oponn.name: Oponn
oponn.description: The Twin gods of Luck and Chance, consisting of the Jester (male, the Pusher who pushes luck into bad directions) and the Lady (female, the Puller who pulls luck favourably). They are capricious, amoral, and their interventions are unpredictable. They affect events through coins and chance encounters. They take an interest in Ganoes Paran early in the series.

d'rek.name: D'rek
d'rek.description: The Worm of Autumn, a goddess associated with decay, cycles, and her own priesthood. She destroys her own worshippers in a terrible purging, an act that devastates the priest Banaschar. She represents the necessary destruction that precedes renewal.

queen of dreams.name: Queen of Dreams
queen of dreams.description: The Goddess of High House Life, associated with the Mockra warren of illusion and mind. She operates through dreams and mental influence, working her designs through the subconscious. She is ancient and enigmatic.

the crippled god (kaminsod).name: The Crippled God (Kaminsod)
the crippled god (kaminsod).description: An alien deity from another realm who was pulled down from the sky by a cabal of mages and chained to the Malazan world. The impact shattered him and his pain radiates poison and corruption into the world. He represents suffering itself — he lashes out because he is in constant agony and wants to spread his misery to others, or alternatively to gather enough power to free himself. Various factions want to use, destroy, or further imprison him. The central revelation of the series is that the Bonehunters' true mission is not to destroy him but to show him compassion and free him from his chains, sending him home. His name is Kaminsod and his nature as both villain and victim is central to the series' themes about compassion and justice.

errastas.name: Errastas
errastas.description: The Errant, an Elder God of chance and manipulation in the Letherii pantheon. He is the oldest active god in the Letherii system, the master of the Tiles of the Holds. He is petty, vengeful, and desperately trying to restore the old power structure of the Holds against the newer warrens. He manipulates events throughout the Letherii books and is one of the series' most despicable divine figures.

kilmandaros.name: Kilmandaros
kilmandaros.description: An Elder Goddess of immense physical power, capable of destroying mountains with her fists. She is the mother of several beings and is allied with other Elder forces seeking to restore the old order. She is primal, ancient, and devastating in direct confrontation.

sechul lath.name: Sechul Lath
sechul lath.description: An Elder God known as Knuckles, associated with chance and the casting of the Tiles of the Holds. He is a schemer who allies with Errastas and Kilmandaros to restore the Holds' dominance. He is dissolute, bitter, and ultimately a pawn in larger games.

grizzin farl.name: Grizzin Farl
grizzin farl.description: A mysterious Elder figure whose role is minor but whose existence hints at deeper layers of the cosmology. He represents the unknown depths of the Elder age.

gothos.name: Gothos
gothos.description: An ancient Jaghut author of the Folly, a legendary and impossibly long text that serves as both history and philosophy. He is reclusive, sardonic, and possessed of a dark humour typical of the Jaghut. He has appeared at key moments throughout history, always as an observer and commentator rather than a participant. He is one of the most enigmatic and entertaining Jaghut in the series.

raest.name: Raest
raest.description: A Jaghut Tyrant who was imprisoned beneath the earth near Darujhistan. He is awakened by Lorn's mission and threatens to destroy the city before being imprisoned by the Finnest House, the Azath of Darujhistan. He is immensely powerful, arrogant, and represents the threat the Jaghut Tyrants once posed to the world. He later becomes a peculiar custodian of the Finnest House.

osseric (osserc).name: Osseric (Osserc)
osseric (osserc).description: A Tiste Liosan Soletaken, son of Father Light, and an ancient and powerful figure in the Tiste cosmology. He is connected to the Warren of Light and has a complex history involving the original sundering of the Tiste into their three factions. He is arrogant, powerful, and involved in events spanning millennia.

scabandari bloodeye.name: Scabandari Bloodeye
scabandari bloodeye.description: A Tiste Edur Soletaken Eleint who betrayed his ally Silchas Ruin in ancient times, stabbing him in the back after they had together defeated the K'Chain Che'Malle. He is the founding ancestor-figure of the Tiste Edur and his betrayal is one of the original sins of the Tiste races. His soul remains a prize sought by various factions.

sheltatha lore.name: Sheltatha Lore
