sheltatha lore.description: A Tiste Soletaken Eleint from the ancient era of Scabandari. She is allied with Sukul Ankhadu and opposes Menandore. The three of them form a dangerous and unstable triangle of power and rivalry. She is ambitious and dangerous.

sukul ankhadu.name: Sukul Ankhadu
sukul ankhadu.description: A Tiste Soletaken Eleint, ally of Sheltatha Lore. She is part of the ancient Tiste conflicts that continue to echo into the present. She is treacherous and power-hungry.

menandore.name: Menandore
menandore.description: A Tiste Soletaken Eleint, opposed to Sheltatha and Sukul. She is connected to Udinaas through the Wyval blood. She is the mother-figure to a child of significance and represents another facet of the ancient Tiste conflicts.

lady envy.name: Lady Envy
lady envy.description: A daughter of Draconus, immensely powerful and capricious. She is beautiful, vain, manipulative, and treats the world as her personal entertainment. She travels with a bizarre retinue including Seguleh warriors and the T'lan Imass Tool. Despite her frivolous exterior she commands godlike power and has lived for millennia. She and her sister Spite despise each other.

lady spite.name: Lady Spite
lady spite.description: Sister of Envy, daughter of Draconus. She is equally powerful but more controlled and less theatrically vain. She operates with more subtlety than her sister. The rivalry between the two sisters is legendary and potentially catastrophic.

dassem ultor.name: Dassem Ultor
dassem ultor.description: The former First Sword of the Malazan Empire, considered the greatest mortal swordsman who ever lived. He served Emperor Kellanved as the supreme champion and military commander. He was betrayed and apparently killed but survived and eventually ascended to become Dessembrae, the Lord of Tragedy. His martial skill is so extraordinary that even Ascendants fear facing him in direct combat.

temper.name: Temper
temper.description: A veteran Malazan soldier who served under Dassem Ultor at the Siege of Y'Ghatan and other battles. He is tough, experienced, and carries the memories of the Empire's most brutal campaigns. He appears primarily in the novels by Ian Cameron Esslemont.

toc the elder.name: Toc the Elder
toc the elder.description: A legendary Malazan commander and father of Toc the Younger. He served in the Empire's early campaigns and is remembered as one of the finest officers of his generation. He disappeared under mysterious circumstances.

nil.name: Nil
nil.description: A young Wickan warlock child, one of a pair of twins who serves Coltaine during the Chain of Dogs. He and his sister Nether provide magical support for the army. He is young but channels considerable power through Wickan spirit magic.

nether.name: Nether
nether.description: Nil's twin sister, a Wickan witch child who serves alongside her brother during the Chain of Dogs. Together they channel the spirits of the Wickan ancestors and the land to aid Coltaine's army. They are both children thrust into the horrors of war.

sormo e'nath.name: Sormo E'nath
sormo e'nath.description: A young Wickan warlock of great power during the Chain of Dogs. He is the most powerful of the Wickan spirit-callers and plays a crucial role in several battles. He is killed during the campaign, a devastating loss to Coltaine's magical capabilities.

bent.name: Bent
bent.description: A huge Wickan cattle dog, a war-dog who survives the Chain of Dogs. He is enormous, fierce, and loyal. He becomes a companion to various characters throughout the series and his survival through impossible circumstances makes him something of a legend.

roach.name: Roach
roach.description: A small, seemingly useless lap-dog who accompanies Bent. The contrast between the massive war-dog and the tiny lap-dog provides comedy. Roach is surprisingly resilient.

curdle.name: Curdle
curdle.description: One of a pair of small skeletal reptilian spirits (former Soletaken Eleint reduced to tiny forms). She and Telorast bicker constantly, scheme incompetently, and provide comic relief. They are cowardly, self-serving, and endlessly entertaining.

telorast.name: Telorast
telorast.description: Curdle's partner, the other small skeleton spirit. Together they are former Soletaken Eleint who have been reduced to absurd, tiny skeletal forms. They argue constantly about everything and serve as reluctant companions to various characters.

pithy.name: Pithy
pithy.description: A Shake Letherii woman who becomes a Watch officer on the Shore during the defence against the Tiste Liosan. She is ordinary in origin but rises to her role through necessity.

brevity.name: Brevity
brevity.description: Pithy's companion, another Shake Letherii woman who serves alongside her on the Shore. Together they represent the common people drawn into extraordinary events.

tiste andii.name: Tiste Andii
tiste andii.description: The Children of Darkness, an Elder race with dark skin and a deep connection to the Warren of Kurald Galain (Darkness). They are led by Anomander Rake and dwell in Moon's Spawn and later Black Coral. They are ancient, long-lived, and suffer from a spiritual malaise after millennia of existence, many having lost the will to live. Their relationship with Mother Dark, their goddess, was severed in antiquity, causing a wound that defines their entire culture.

tiste edur.name: Tiste Edur
tiste edur.description: The Children of Shadow, grey-skinned Elder race connected to Kurald Emurlahn, the Warren of Shadow. They live on the Lether continent and are a warrior culture with tribal structures. They are corrupted by the Crippled God through Rhulad's cursed sword and invade Lether, becoming its rulers. They were once part of the unified Tiste before the ancient sundering.

tiste liosan.name: Tiste Liosan
tiste liosan.description: The Children of Light, an Elder race connected to Kurald Thyrllan, the Warren of Light. They are fanatical, aggressive, and believe in their absolute superiority. They launch invasions through breaches in reality and are the primary threat at the Shore. They are the least sympathetically portrayed of the three Tiste factions.

jaghut.name: Jaghut
jaghut.description: An ancient Elder race characterised by tusked faces and immense sorcerous power, particularly ice magic (Omtose Phellack). They are mostly solitary beings who refuse to form civilisations, a deliberate choice made in response to the danger of Jaghut Tyrants — individual Jaghut who seize power over others. They were hunted nearly to extinction by the T'lan Imass. They are sardonic, darkly humorous, and philosophical. Hood was originally a Jaghut.

t'lan imass.name: T'lan Imass
t'lan imass.description: The Imass people who underwent the Ritual of Tellann over three hundred thousand years ago, transforming themselves into undying skeletal warriors dedicated to the eternal war against the Jaghut. They are desiccated, fleshless, and sustained by the Warren of Tellann. They can disintegrate into dust and reform. They retain their memories but have lost the capacity for emotion over the millennia, making them tragic figures. Their Bonecasters are their spiritual leaders.

imass.name: Imass
imass.description: The mortal ancestors of humans who predated civilisation, the original people before they underwent the Ritual of Tellann. Living Imass were hunter-gatherers who waged war against the Jaghut. They are the precursors to both the T'lan Imass and modern humanity.

forkrul assail.name: Forkrul Assail
forkrul assail.description: An Elder race obsessed with balance and a twisted concept of justice. They are extremely dangerous, possessing the Voice — a supernatural ability to command obedience through speech. They are the primary antagonists of the final books, having conquered Kolanse and drained the land of life. They believe they are restoring cosmic balance but their methods are genocidal.

k'chain che'malle.name: K'Chain Che'Malle
k'chain che'malle.description: An ancient reptilian race predating most others, builders of massive sky keeps (flying fortresses). They are divided into castes: Matrons (rulers), K'ell Hunters (warriors), Ve'Gath (heavy warriors), and Shi'gal Assassins (aerial killers). They were nearly destroyed in ancient wars but remnants survive. In the final books, some ally with the Bonehunters.

k'chain nah'ruk.name: K'Chain Nah'ruk
k'chain nah'ruk.description: An offshoot of the K'Chain Che'Malle, smaller, short-tailed, and more aggressive. They are an enemy faction that launches devastating attacks. Their battle against the Bonehunters is one of the most visceral combat sequences in the series.

barghast.name: Barghast
barghast.description: A warlike semi-nomadic people descended in part from the Imass. They are divided into clans collectively known as the White Face Barghast. They are large, fierce, and honour-bound. Their culture values martial prowess and ancestral spirits. They play significant roles in the Pannion War and later events.

moranth.name: Moranth
moranth.description: A secretive people living in mountain city-states who are divided into colour-coded clans (Black, Blue, Green, Gold, Silver, Red). They manufacture alchemical munitions that have revolutionised warfare and ride insectoid creatures called quorls for aerial transport. Their alliance with the Malazan Empire provides crucial military advantages.

wickan.name: Wickan
wickan.description: A horse-warrior people from the Wickan Plains of Quon Tali. They are fierce riders, skilled in mounted combat, and have a rich tradition of spirit magic channelled through their warlocks and witches. Coltaine is their most famous representative. They are loyal, proud, and devastating in cavalry warfare.

trell.name: Trell
trell.description: A large, furred humanoid race distantly related to the Imass. They are physically powerful, with broad features and body hair. Mappo Runt is the most prominent Trell. They are a declining people whose numbers have diminished over the millennia.

toblakai.name: Toblakai
toblakai.description: A giant warrior race descended from the ancient Thel Akai. They stand well over seven feet tall and are immensely strong. Karsa Orlong is the most famous Toblakai. The name encompasses several related peoples including the Teblor.

teblor.name: Teblor
teblor.description: Northern mountain tribes of Toblakai descent, Karsa Orlong's people. They live in isolated communities and have been deliberately kept ignorant of the wider world. Their gods are actually imprisoned Forkrul Assail. They are fierce warriors with a culture built around raiding and honour.

thel akai.name: Thel Akai
thel akai.description: An ancient giant Elder race, the original ancestors of the Toblakai, Teblor, and Thelomen lines. They were among the first peoples and their bloodline runs through several of the world's giant races.

thelomen.name: Thelomen
thelomen.description: A giant race related to the Toblakai line. Bellurdan was a Thelomen. They are large, powerful, and rare in the modern era of the series.

thelomen toblakai.name: Thelomen Toblakai
thelomen toblakai.description: Mixed-descent giants combining Thelomen and Toblakai bloodlines. They represent the interbreeding between the various giant races.

jhag.name: Jhag
jhag.description: Offspring of Jaghut and another race (typically Imass). Icarium is the most famous Jhag. They inherit traits from both parent races, including Jaghut longevity and tusks alongside more human features.

soletaken.name: Soletaken
soletaken.description: Shapeshifters who can assume a single animal form through warren magic. The transformation is powered by accessing a specific warren, usually Starvald Demelain. Soletaken retain their intelligence in animal form. Anomander Rake (dragon), Kilava (panther), and many others are Soletaken. A being capable of shapeshifting into one animal form via warren magic. The transformation is permanent in the sense that the being can shift back and forth at will. Many powerful individuals are Soletaken, including Anomander Rake (dragon) and Kilava (panther).

d'ivers.name: D'ivers
d'ivers.description: Shapeshifters who can split into multiple animal forms simultaneously through warren magic. Unlike Soletaken who become one creature, D'ivers fragment into many (rats, birds, insects, etc.). They are considered more unstable and dangerous than Soletaken, as the division of consciousness can lead to madness. A being who fragments into many animal forms simultaneously via warren magic. Unlike Soletaken who become one creature, D'ivers split into swarms of rats, flights of birds, packs of wolves, etc. The division of consciousness can lead to instability and madness.

eleint.name: Eleint
eleint.description: True dragons, immensely powerful beings with their own Warren (Starvald Demelain). They are distinct from Soletaken who take dragon form, being genuine dragons from birth. They are among the most powerful beings in the world and their blood carries transformative properties.

eres'al.name: Eres'al
eres'al.description: An extremely ancient proto-human spirit, ancestral to the Imass and therefore to humanity. She appears as a prehistoric hominid and possesses vast spiritual power. She restores Onrack to mortal flesh and represents the deepest roots of human existence.

shake.name: Shake
shake.description: A coastal people with ancient ties to the Shore, the boundary between the realms of Light and Darkness. They are led by Yan Tovis (Twilight) and defended by Yedan Derryg (The Watch). Their destiny is to guard the breach between worlds.

letherii.name: Letherii
letherii.description: A civilised mercantile people of the Lether continent who are obsessed with debt, economic competition, and material wealth. Their society uses indentured servitude and debt-slavery as fundamental institutions. They are conquered by the Tiste Edur but their economic system proves more persistent than military rule.

nerek.name: Nerek
nerek.description: A subject people conquered and exploited by the Letherii. They represent the human cost of Letherii expansionism.

faraed.name: Faraed
faraed.description: Another subject people within the Letherii empire, similarly conquered and diminished.

tarthenal.name: Tarthenal
tarthenal.description: Toblakai-descended people within Letherii territory who have been reduced and conquered. They retain some of their ancestors' size but have been culturally broken by Letherii domination.

bolkando.name: Bolkando
bolkando.description: A kingdom neighbouring Lether, ruled by a queen. They have a complex relationship with both the Letherii and the Bonehunters' passage through their territory.

saphinand.name: Saphinand
saphinand.description: A people and kingdom near Bolkando, part of the political landscape of the Lether continent.

akrynnai.name: Akrynnai
akrynnai.description: Horse-riding pastoral people of the Lether continent who are drawn into the conflicts of the later books. They are skilled riders and herders.

khundryl.name: Khundryl
khundryl.description: Fierce mounted warriors from Seven Cities. The Burned Tears clan under Warleader Gall swears loyalty to Adjunct Tavore and joins the Bonehunters' campaign. They are proud, honour-bound, and devastating in cavalry warfare.

burned tears.name: Burned Tears
burned tears.description: The specific Khundryl clan that joins the Bonehunters. Their name derives from a ritual of mourning and their devotion to their oaths is absolute.

perish grey helms.name: Perish Grey Helms
perish grey helms.description: A religious military order devoted to the Wolves of Winter (Togg and Fanderay). They are heavily armoured, disciplined, and organised along religious lines with a Destriant, Mortal Sword, and Shield Anvil. They ally with the Bonehunters but are partially corrupted by a Forkrul Assail infiltrator. A military order devoted to the Wolves of Winter (Togg and Fanderay). They are heavily armoured, disciplined, and organised along religious lines with a triumvirate of Destriant, Mortal Sword, and Shield Anvil. They ally with the Bonehunters but are partially corrupted by a Forkrul Assail infiltrator.

bonehunters.name: Bonehunters
bonehunters.description: Adjunct Tavore's army, reconstituted from the survivors of the Seven Cities campaign. They are named after surviving the battle of Y'Ghatan, where they tunnelled through the burning city. They become the instrument of the Crippled God's liberation. They are characterised by stubborn determination and loyalty to each other despite not understanding their Adjunct's goals. Adjunct Tavore's reconstituted army after the disaster at Y'Ghatan in Seven Cities. They earned their name after surviving the burning of Y'Ghatan by tunnelling through the city's foundations, emerging from the bones of the dead beneath the streets. They march across continents and ultimately free the Crippled God. They represent the common soldier's endurance and loyalty.

bridgeburners.name: Bridgeburners
bridgeburners.description: The most legendary company in the Malazan military, founded during the Emperor's early campaigns. They earned their name by burning the bridges behind enemy lines. They are the Empire's most storied soldiers, including Whiskeyjack, Fiddler, Quick Ben, Kalam, and many others. They are deliberately destroyed by Empress Laseen and the survivors become legends. The most elite and legendary company in the Malazan military, founded during Emperor Kellanved's early campaigns. They earned their name by burning the bridges behind enemy lines, cutting off any possibility of retreat. They served through every major campaign of the Empire's expansion. The company includes some of the series' most important characters: Whiskeyjack, Fiddler, Quick Ben, Kalam, Hedge, Picker, Blend, Antsy, and many others. They are deliberately destroyed by Empress Laseen's political machinations, but their legend endures and their surviving members become near-mythical figures.

crimson guard.name: Crimson Guard
crimson guard.description: A mercenary company whose Avowed members swore a magical vow to oppose the Malazan Empire forever. The vow grants them near-immortality but binds them to an eternal war. They are formidable fighters and their conflict with the Empire spans the entire series. A mercenary company whose core members, the Avowed, swore a magical vow to oppose the Malazan Empire forever. The vow grants them near-immortality and supernatural resilience. They are led by K'azz D'Avore and include many formidable warriors. Their eternal war against the Empire is a recurring element throughout the series.

malazan.name: Malazan
malazan.description: Citizens and soldiers of the Malazan Empire, the dominant political and military power in much of the world. The Empire was founded by Kellanved and Dancer on Malaz Island and expanded through military conquest and political cunning.

genabackan.name: Genabackan
genabackan.description: People from the continent of Genabackis, the setting for much of the early series. Genabackis hosts many independent cities and nations that the Malazan Empire is attempting to conquer.

seven cities.name: Seven Cities
seven cities.description: People from the Seven Cities subcontinent, a desert region with diverse cultures united by shared history and periodic rebellion against Malazan rule. The Whirlwind rebellion originates here. A subcontinent east of Quon Tali, a vast desert region with ancient civilisations. It is the setting for the Whirlwind rebellion and the Chain of Dogs. Its peoples have been conquered by the Malazan Empire but retain a fierce desire for independence.

nathii.name: Nathii
nathii.description: A Genabackan people encountered during the campaigns on that continent.

rhivi.name: Rhivi
rhivi.description: Nomadic people of the Rhivi Plain in Genabackis who herd bhederin (large bison-like creatures). They are allied with Caladan Brood against the Malazan Empire. Silverfox is born among them.

semk.name: Semk
semk.description: A tribal warrior people of Seven Cities who join the Whirlwind rebellion. They are fierce fighters.

anibar.name: Anibar
anibar.description: Desert people of Seven Cities, one of the many cultures of that subcontinent.

kanese.name: Kanese
kanese.description: People from Kan, a Malazan province on Quon Tali. Apsalar is originally from a Kanese fishing village.

kartoolian.name: Kartoolian
kartoolian.description: People from Kartool, an island known for its spider cult. The island has a reputation for strangeness and danger.

stormriders.name: Stormriders
stormriders.description: Mysterious alien beings who assault the Stormwall of Korel from the sea. They are elemental creatures of water and ice, utterly alien and relentlessly hostile. The Stormwall is maintained by prisoners forced to fight them.

azath.name: Azath
azath.description: Sentient houses that grow from the ground to imprison powerful beings, maintaining cosmic balance. They appear across the world and seem to serve a fundamental regulatory function in the universe. They are not a race but a phenomenon, though they exhibit intelligence and purpose. Sentient structures that grow from the ground to imprison powerful beings, maintaining cosmic balance. They appear worldwide and seem to serve as a natural check on excessive power.

darujhistan.name: Darujhistan
darujhistan.description: The great free city on the south shore of Lake Azur in Genabackis, known for its blue-gas-lit streets, its eccentric citizens, and its fierce independence. It successfully resists Malazan conquest through a combination of political scheming, magical intervention, and sheer luck. It is one of the most richly detailed settings in the series.

pale.name: Pale
pale.description: A Genabackan city conquered by the Malazan Empire after the devastating Siege of Pale, during which Moon's Spawn was engaged by imperial mages. The battle kills many and sets the events of the first book in motion.

genabackis.name: Genabackis
genabackis.description: A large northern continent and the setting for the first, third, and eighth books. It hosts numerous independent cities, the Pannion Domin, and is the primary theatre of the Malazan Empire's northern campaigns.

quon tali.name: Quon Tali
quon tali.description: The central continent of the Malazan Empire and the seat of imperial power. It includes the capital Unta and numerous provinces. It is where the Empire originated and where much of its political drama unfolds.

malaz city.name: Malaz City
malaz city.description: The port city on Malaz Island where the Malazan Empire was founded by Kellanved and Dancer. It remains an important but somewhat rough-and-tumble location, home to the original Deadhouse (Azath).

malaz island.name: Malaz Island
malaz island.description: The small island off the coast of Quon Tali where the Empire began. It is significant beyond its size because of the Deadhouse and its historical importance.

mock's hold.name: Mock's Hold
mock's hold.description: The fortress on Malaz Island, originally held by the pirate Mock before Kellanved seized it. It overlooks the city and is the site of several important events.

unta.name: Unta
unta.description: The capital city of the Malazan Empire on Quon Tali. It is the seat of the Empress and the centre of imperial administration, a large and politically complex city.

aren.name: Aren
aren.description: The largest city of Seven Cities, a major port on the southern coast. It is the site of Coltaine's Fall, where the Chain of Dogs refugees are massacred within sight of the city walls. The garrison's failure to open the gates is one of the series' most infuriating moments.

ehrlitan.name: Ehrlitan
ehrlitan.description: A holy city of Seven Cities, one of the major urban centres of the subcontinent.

hissar.name: Hissar
hissar.description: A Seven Cities port city where the Whirlwind rebellion first erupts into open warfare.

raraku.name: Raraku
raraku.description: The Holy Desert of Seven Cities, a vast wasteland that conceals an ancient sea beneath its sands. It is the spiritual centre of the Whirlwind rebellion and a place of profound supernatural significance. Beneath its sands lie the remnants of a vast inland sea.

y'ghatan.name: Y'Ghatan
y'ghatan.description: An ancient fortress city in Seven Cities, scene of two famous last stands. The second battle of Y'Ghatan sees the Bonehunters trapped in a burning city, surviving by tunnelling through the foundations.

g'danisban.name: G'danisban
g'danisban.description: A Seven Cities settlement known for its debauched and addicted population, devastated by the plague goddess Poliel.

letheras.name: Letheras
letheras.description: The capital of the Letherii kingdom, a wealthy city built on canals. It is characterised by its obsession with commerce and debt. Its conquest by the Tiste Edur and later reconstruction form a major storyline.

lether.name: Lether
lether.description: The continent east of the main landmasses, home of the Letherii and the Tiste Edur. It is the setting for the fifth, seventh, and parts of later books.

kolanse.name: Kolanse
kolanse.description: A distant eastern land conquered by the Forkrul Assail, who drain it of life. It is the final destination of the Bonehunters' march and the setting of the series' climactic battle.

kharkanas.name: Kharkanas
kharkanas.description: The ancient city of the Tiste Andii, the citadel of Darkness itself. It is the setting of the prequel Kharkanas trilogy and represents the origins of Tiste civilisation before the sundering.

emurlahn.name: Emurlahn
emurlahn.description: The shattered Warren of Shadow, the Tiste Edur homeland in magical terms. It was broken in ancient warfare and exists as fragments.

kurald galain.name: Kurald Galain
kurald galain.description: The Elder Warren of Darkness, the home realm of the Tiste Andii. It is the source of their sorcerous power and the domain of Mother Dark. The Elder Warren of Darkness, the oldest and deepest source of dark sorcery. It is the home realm of the Tiste Andii and the domain of Mother Dark. Practitioners channel shadow, cold, and negation.

kurald emurlahn.name: Kurald Emurlahn
kurald emurlahn.description: The Elder Warren of Shadow, shattered in ancient war. Its fragments form the basis of the Shadow Realm. The Elder Warren of Shadow, broken into fragments by ancient warfare between the Tiste factions. Its shattered state is reflected in the fragmented nature of the Shadow Realm.

kurald thyrllan.name: Kurald Thyrllan
kurald thyrllan.description: The Elder Warren of Light, realm of the Tiste Liosan. It is the source of Light magic and the home of Father Light. The Elder Warren of Light, the source of Tiste Liosan power. It is the domain of Father Light and generates radiant energy magic.

omtose phellack.name: Omtose Phellack
omtose phellack.description: The Elder Jaghut Warren of Ice, associated with stasis, cold, and the freezing of time. It is the source of Jaghut sorcery and has been used to seal away threats. The Elder Jaghut Warren of Ice, associated with cold, stasis, and the freezing of time itself. The Jaghut used it to create ice ages and to seal away threats. It is declining in power as the Jaghut dwindle.

tellann.name: Tellann
tellann.description: The Warren of the T'lan Imass, associated with fire and the Ritual of undeath. It is the power source that sustains the T'lan Imass in their skeletal forms. The Warren of fire and the T'lan Imass. It powers the Ritual that transformed the Imass into undead warriors. It can generate flame and is the source of the T'lan Imass's supernatural endurance.

starvald demelain.name: Starvald Demelain
starvald demelain.description: The First Warren, the realm of the Eleint (true dragons) and the source from which all other warrens descended. It is the oldest and most fundamental magical dimension. The First Warren, the draconic source realm from which all other warrens originated. It is the home of the Eleint and contains the raw, primal essence of magical power. All warrens are diluted versions of this original source.

shadow realm (meanas).name: Shadow Realm (Meanas)
shadow realm (meanas).description: The Warren of illusion and shadow, ruled by Shadowthrone and Cotillion. It is populated by shadow demons and serves as a base of operations for the Shadow gods.

imperial warren.name: Imperial Warren
imperial warren.description: A lifeless grey wasteland used for rapid military transport across the Malazan Empire. It is artificial, possibly created or shaped by Kellanved, and allows armies to travel vast distances quickly. An artificial warren used for fast military travel across the Malazan Empire. It appears as a grey, lifeless wasteland. It was created or discovered by Kellanved and is a key military asset.

azath house.name: Azath House
azath house.description: Any of the sentient structures that trap Ascendants and powerful beings. They appear worldwide and serve a cosmic regulatory function, preventing any single power from becoming too dominant.

deadhouse.name: Deadhouse
deadhouse.description: The Azath House on Malaz Island, one of the most important structures in the series. It plays a role in Kellanved and Dancer's ascension and holds many powerful prisoners.

finnest house.name: Finnest House
finnest house.description: The Azath House in Darujhistan, which imprisons the Jaghut Tyrant Raest. It stands as a garden-covered house in the city, deceptively ordinary in appearance.

tremorlor.name: Tremorlor
tremorlor.description: The Azath House in the desert of Raraku. It is reached through a perilous journey through warrens and serves to contain threats in the Seven Cities region.

black coral.name: Black Coral
black coral.description: A city in Genabackis that becomes the home of the Tiste Andii after Moon's Spawn crashes. It is shrouded in the darkness of Kurald Galain and ruled by the Andii. It becomes the site of major events in the later books.

coral.name: Coral
coral.description: A Genabackan coastal city that is the final battleground against the Pannion Domin. The battle of Coral is one of the major military engagements of the series.

capustan.name: Capustan
capustan.description: A Genabackan city besieged by the Pannion Domin's forces, defended by the Grey Swords and a mixed garrison. The siege is brutal and leads to Itkovian's legendary act of compassion.

pannion domin.name: Pannion Domin
pannion domin.description: A theocratic empire in southeast Genabackis ruled by the Pannion Seer. It is characterised by religious fanaticism, cannibalism (the Tenescowri), and expansionist warfare. It is defeated by the combined alliance of Dujek, Rake, and Brood.

moon's spawn.name: Moon's Spawn
moon's spawn.description: A massive floating mountain fortress of the Tiste Andii, commanded by Anomander Rake. It is ancient, pitted with craters from magical battles, and serves as both home and weapon. It is damaged at the Siege of Pale and eventually crashes at Coral.

drift avalii.name: Drift Avalii
drift avalii.description: A wandering island that holds a Throne of Shadow. It drifts through the oceans and is the site of a confrontation over control of the Shadow throne.

otataral island.name: Otataral Island
otataral island.description: The source of otataral, a magic-deadening ore. It is also home to brutal mining camps where political prisoners and slaves are sent. Felisin Paran is sent here.

glass desert.name: Glass Desert
glass desert.description: A vast barren expanse on the approach to Kolanse that is lethal to cross. The Bonehunters must march through it without adequate supplies, and the Snake (column of refugee children) crosses it as well. It is one of the most gruelling trials in the series.

lake azur.name: Lake Azur
lake azur.description: A large lake in Genabackis on whose southern shores the city of Darujhistan sits.

mott wood.name: Mott Wood
mott wood.description: A dense forest in Genabackis, home to the Mott Irregulars, a guerrilla fighting force known for their eccentric methods.

blackdog forest.name: Blackdog Forest
blackdog forest.description: A forest in Genabackis featured in military campaigns.

morn.name: Morn
morn.description: A ruined K'Chain Che'Malle sky keep that crashed in Genabackis in ancient times. It contains a rent (tear) in reality and is a dangerous and significant location.

meanas.name: Meanas
meanas.description: The Warren of Shadow and Illusion, the mortal-accessible aspect of the shattered Kurald Emurlahn. It is used for creating illusions, deceptions, and shadow magic. It is ruled by Shadowthrone and Cotillion.

rashan.name: Rashan
rashan.description: The Warren of Darkness accessible to mortal mages, a diluted form of Kurald Galain. It generates shadows, darkness, and negation effects.

thyr.name: Thyr
thyr.description: The Warren of Light, used for illumination, energy attacks, and light-based sorcery. Tattersail was a practitioner.

telas.name: Telas
telas.description: The Warren of Fire, the source of destructive flame magic. It can generate devastating conflagrations and was the warren that transformed Gesler and Stormy.

denul.name: Denul
denul.description: The Warren of Healing, used by military healers, physicians, and those who mend wounds and cure illness. It is one of the most valued warrens in military service. Mallet was a Denul practitioner.

d'riss.name: D'riss
d'riss.description: The Warren of Stone and Earth, associated with paths, the deep ground, and geological forces. It can generate stone constructs and manipulate earth.

ruse.name: Ruse
ruse.description: The Warren of the Sea, oceanic magic that governs tides, currents, and water. It is used by naval mages and those connected to the ocean.

serc.name: Serc
serc.description: The Warren of Air and Sky, used for wind manipulation, flight, and atmospheric effects.

mockra.name: Mockra
mockra.description: The Warren of the Mind, used for illusion, mental manipulation, and deceiving the senses. It can create false perceptions and manipulate thoughts. The Queen of Dreams is associated with it.

hood's path.name: Hood's Path
hood's path.description: The Warren of Death, connecting the living world to the realm of the dead. It is the pathway spirits take and can be used to communicate with or summon the dead.

otataral.name: Otataral
otataral.description: An anti-magic ore that negates sorcery in its vicinity. It is mined on Otataral Island and fashioned into weapons and tools for fighting mages. Exposure to it is harmful to magic users. The Adjuncts' swords are made of otataral. A rare ore found primarily on Otataral Island that nullifies magic in its vicinity. It is deadly to mages and is fashioned into weapons, chains, and tools for combating sorcery. The Adjuncts' swords are made of otataral.

akhrast korvalain.name: Akhrast Korvalain
akhrast korvalain.description: The Elder Warren of the Forkrul Assail, the source of their supernatural Voice and their abilities of compulsion. It is one of the most dangerous Elder Warrens.

elder warrens.name: Elder Warrens
elder warrens.description: The original magical realms that predate the mortal warrens. They are far more powerful and fundamental, including Kurald Galain, Omtose Phellack, Tellann, and others. Mortal warrens are diluted versions of these.

holds.name: Holds
holds.description: An older system of magical power predating the warrens, used primarily in Letherii culture. They are accessed through Tiles rather than cards and represent more primal aspects of reality.

beast hold.name: Beast Hold
beast hold.description: The Hold associated with animal spirits and primal natural forces.

azath hold.name: Azath Hold
azath hold.description: The Hold connected to the Azath Houses and their regulatory function.

empty hold.name: Empty Hold
empty hold.description: A Hold representing absence, vacancy, and potential. It was later filled as new powers emerged.

ice hold.name: Ice Hold
ice hold.description: The Hold of cold and Jaghut-related power, connected to Omtose Phellack.

fire hold.name: Fire Hold
fire hold.description: The Hold of flame and destructive/transformative fire.

life hold.name: Life Hold
life hold.description: The Hold associated with living forces, growth, and vitality.

death hold.name: Death Hold
death hold.description: The Hold associated with dying, the dead, and the transition between life and death.

claw.name: Claw
claw.description: The Malazan Empire's secret intelligence and assassination service, founded by Surly (Laseen) before she became Empress. They are the Empire's eyes, ears, and daggers, trained to kill through stealth, poison, and sorcery. They replaced the older Talon organisation.

talon.name: Talon
talon.description: A predecessor/rival secret organisation to the Claw, believed destroyed by Laseen during her rise to power. They served Emperor Kellanved and some agents survived in hiding. Baudin was a Talon agent.

malazan marines.name: Malazan Marines
malazan marines.description: The amphibious shock troops of the Malazan military, trained for close assault, city fighting, and beach landings. They are considered the toughest and most versatile troops in the imperial army.

grey swords.name: Grey Swords
grey swords.description: A religious mercenary company devoted initially to Fener (and later the Wolves of Winter). They defend Capustan against the Pannion Domin and are largely destroyed in the siege. Their Shield Anvil Itkovian performs one of the series' most memorable acts of compassion.

khundryl burned tears.name: Khundryl Burned Tears
khundryl burned tears.description: A mounted warrior clan from Seven Cities who swear loyalty to Adjunct Tavore and join the Bonehunters' campaign. They are devastating cavalry under Warleader Gall.

barghast white face clans.name: Barghast White Face clans
barghast white face clans.description: The united Barghast clans under war-leaders who participate in the Pannion War and later events. They are fierce, proud, and bound by complex clan loyalties.

mott irregulars.name: Mott Irregulars
mott irregulars.description: Guerrilla fighters from Mott Wood in Genabackis. They are eccentric, unconventional, and surprisingly effective. Their methods are unorthodox and often comedic.

pannion domin forces.name: Pannion Domin forces
pannion domin forces.description: The armies of the theocratic Pannion Seer, including regular troops, religious fanatics, and the Tenescowri.

tenescowri.name: Tenescowri
tenescowri.description: The Pannion Domin's peasant army, composed of starving civilians driven by religious frenzy and reduced to cannibalism. They are horrifying not because they are skilled but because of their sheer numbers and the desperation that drives them.

logros t'lan imass.name: Logros T'lan Imass
logros t'lan imass.description: The clan of T'lan Imass that served the Malazan Empire directly. They are one of several Imass armies scattered across the world.

kron t'lan imass.name: Kron T'lan Imass
kron t'lan imass.description: Another T'lan Imass clan army, among the original Imass who underwent the Ritual of Tellann.

ifayle t'lan imass.name: Ifayle T'lan Imass
ifayle t'lan imass.description: A T'lan Imass clan that was scattered and lost over the millennia.

fist.name: Fist
fist.description: A general-rank officer in the Malazan military who commands a division of several thousand soldiers. Equivalent to a modern brigadier general.

high fist.name: High Fist
high fist.description: The highest active military rank in the Malazan Empire, commanding an entire theatre of operations. Only a handful hold this rank at any time. Dujek Onearm is the most prominent.

adjunct.name: Adjunct
adjunct.description: The direct representative of the Empress, who commands independently with supreme authority. The Adjunct answers only to the Empress and can override any military or civilian authority. Lorn and Tavore both hold this title.

empress.name: Empress
empress.description: The ruler of the Malazan Empire. During the events of the series this is Laseen, who seized the throne from Kellanved.

ceda.name: Ceda
ceda.description: The chief mage of the Letherii kingdom, the highest sorcerous authority. Kuru Qan holds this position. It is roughly equivalent to a Malazan High Mage but carries political as well as magical authority.

preda.name: Preda
preda.description: A military commander rank in Letherii society, equivalent to a general.

atri-preda.name: Atri-Preda
atri-preda.description: A senior military rank in the Letherii forces, equivalent to a Malazan Fist. Yan Tovis holds this rank.

bonecaster.name: Bonecaster
bonecaster.description: A shaman-mage of the Imass or T'lan Imass who wields Tellann magic. Bonecasters are the spiritual leaders and most powerful magic users of their people. Pran Chole, Kilava, and Olar Ethil are all Bonecasters.

ascendant.name: Ascendant
ascendant.description: A being who has gained immense power approaching godhood through worship, deed, or magical transformation. Ascendants are not quite gods but are far beyond mortal. Many characters in the series ascend over its course.

high house.name: High House
high house.description: One of the divine houses in the Deck of Dragons, each representing a sphere of power (Death, Shadow, Life, etc.). Each High House has positions such as King, Queen, Knight, and others, filled by Ascendants and gods.

deck of dragons.name: Deck of Dragons
deck of dragons.description: A divinatory card system reflecting the current state of Ascendants, gods, and magical power in the world. Reading the Deck is dangerous, as it draws the attention of the powers depicted. Fiddler has a natural talent for reading the Deck. It replaced the older Tiles of the Holds.

finnest.name: Finnest
finnest.description: A receptacle containing the concentrated power of a Jaghut Tyrant. It is an object (usually organic, like an acorn) that holds the Tyrant's bound power. Destroying a Finnest is extremely difficult and dangerous.

moranth munitions.name: Moranth munitions
moranth munitions.description: Alchemical explosive weapons crafted by the Moranth people. They have revolutionised warfare in the Malazan world, giving the Empire a decisive technological advantage. They come in several types including cussers, sharpers, burners, smokers, crackers, and whistlers.

cusser.name: Cusser
cusser.description: The largest and most destructive Moranth munition, capable of levelling buildings and killing everything in a wide area. Sappers treat them with extreme caution (usually).

sharper.name: Sharper
sharper.description: A standard-issue Moranth explosive munition, equivalent to a fragmentation grenade. It shatters into razor-sharp pieces on detonation.

burner.name: Burner
burner.description: A Moranth incendiary munition that creates intense, clinging fire upon detonation.

smoker.name: Smoker
smoker.description: A Moranth munition that produces thick, obscuring smoke for concealment and confusion.

cracker.name: Cracker
cracker.description: A Moranth flash-bang munition that creates concussive noise and blinding light, used to disorient enemies.

whistler.name: Whistler
whistler.description: A Moranth munition that shrieks in flight, used for signalling or psychological warfare against enemies.

warren.name: Warren
warren.description: A magical dimension that mages access to draw sorcerous power. Each warren has a different character (fire, ice, healing, death, etc.) and accessing one requires training and talent. The warrens were created by the blood of K'rul.

hold.name: Hold
hold.description: An older magical power system predating the warrens, accessed through the casting of Tiles rather than reading cards. The Holds represent more primal aspects of reality and are the dominant magical framework in Letherii culture.

ascendancy.name: Ascendancy
ascendancy.description: The state or process of becoming an Ascendant. It typically involves accumulating power through worship, great deeds, or magical transformation until one transcends ordinary mortality.

convergence.name: Convergence
convergence.description: An event where multiple powerful beings and forces are drawn together, often catastrophically. Convergences occur when the Deck of Dragons shifts significantly, and they typically result in massive conflicts.

t'lan.name: T'lan
t'lan.description: A prefix denoting undeath through the Ritual of Tellann. T'lan Imass are Imass who underwent the Ritual. The word literally means "flint" in the Imass language.

ritual of tellann.name: Ritual of Tellann
ritual of tellann.description: The great sorcery performed over three hundred thousand years ago that transformed the living Imass into undying T'lan Imass. It was conceived as a weapon in the eternal war against the Jaghut, granting the Imass immortality at the cost of flesh, emotion, and living warmth.

rent.name: Rent
rent.description: A tear in reality between warrens or realms. Rents are dangerous, unstable, and can release whatever is on the other side. They can be naturally occurring or created by powerful sorcery.

gate.name: Gate
gate.description: A stable portal between realms, warrens, or locations. Gates are more controlled than rents and can be maintained for regular travel.

throne.name: Throne
throne.description: A seat of divine or Ascendant power within a warren or Hold. Controlling a Throne gives one authority over the associated realm. Thrones are contested prizes.

tiles of the holds.name: Tiles of the Holds
tiles of the holds.description: The Letherii divinatory system, predecessor to the Deck of Dragons. Tiles are cast and read rather than drawn as cards. They represent the Holds rather than the High Houses.

cedance.name: Cedance
cedance.description: The chamber in Letheras where the Tiles of the Holds are read by the Ceda. It is a sacred and dangerous place.

master of the deck.name: Master of the Deck
master of the deck.description: A mortal who commands authority over the Deck of Dragons, able to rearrange the positions of power within it. Ganoes Paran assumes this role.

king in chains.name: King in Chains
king in chains.description: A title referring to the Crippled God in his imprisoned, suffering state. The chains that bind him are both physical and metaphysical.

crippled god.name: Crippled God
crippled god.description: See character entry above. A god pulled from another world and chained, the source of corruption and suffering, and ultimately the object of the Bonehunters' compassion.

chaining.name: Chaining
chaining.description: The ancient act of pulling the Crippled God from his own realm to the Malazan world and binding him there. It was performed by a cabal of mages and is the original sin that drives much of the series' cosmic conflict.

jade strangers.name: Jade Strangers
jade strangers.description: Massive jade meteors or statues that fall from the sky, connected to the Crippled God's power. They are fragments of his original realm that followed him when he was pulled down. They radiate an alien energy.

sapper.name: Sapper
sapper.description: A military engineer specialising in demolitions and tunnel work, using Moranth munitions. Sappers are considered both essential and slightly insane by other soldiers. Fiddler and Hedge are the most famous sappers.

healer.name: Healer
healer.description: A Denul warren practitioner who mends wounds and cures illness. Military healers are vital assets and are protected by their comrades.

squad mage.name: Squad Mage
squad mage.description: A mage assigned to a military squad, providing magical support at the tactical level. Quick Ben and Bottle serve in this capacity.

cadre mage.name: Cadre Mage
cadre mage.description: A more senior military mage attached to a larger formation such as a company or battalion. Tattersail served as a cadre mage.

high mage.name: High Mage
high mage.description: The most powerful rank of military or imperial mage. High Mages command vast sorcerous power and serve in advisory roles to the highest commanders. Tayschrenn is the most prominent.

destriant.name: Destriant
destriant.description: The priest role in a religious military triumvirate (Destriant, Mortal Sword, Shield Anvil). The Destriant channels divine healing and spiritual guidance.

mortal sword.name: Mortal Sword
mortal sword.description: The supreme warrior champion of a god in a religious military order. The Mortal Sword is the divine sword arm in the mortal world. Gruntle serves as Mortal Sword of Treach.

shield anvil.name: Shield Anvil
shield anvil.description: The member of a religious triumvirate who absorbs the suffering and grief of others, acting as a vessel for compassion. Itkovian is the most famous Shield Anvil.

pannion seer.name: Pannion Seer
pannion seer.description: The theocratic ruler of the Pannion Domin, a mortal vessel possessed by a Jaghut spirit. He rules through religious terror and military conquest.

seguleh.name: Seguleh
seguleh.description: A masked warrior society from a remote island where rank is determined entirely by duelling. The lower the number on a warrior's mask, the deadlier they are. The First is the supreme fighter. They are considered among the most dangerous warriors in the world.

first (seguleh rank).name: First (Seguleh rank)
first (seguleh rank).description: The supreme fighter of the Seguleh, the single best warrior in their society. Bearing the mask with a single line.

second (seguleh rank).name: Second (Seguleh rank)
second (seguleh rank).description: The second-ranked Seguleh warrior, who has been defeated only by the First.

third (seguleh rank).name: Third (Seguleh rank)
third (seguleh rank).description: The third-ranked Seguleh warrior. The Third appears as a character in the series and is devastatingly skilled.

quorl.name: Quorl
quorl.description: A large flying insectoid creature ridden by the Moranth as aerial mounts. They enable rapid aerial transport and reconnaissance.

black moranth.name: Black Moranth
black moranth.description: The Moranth clan specialising in munitions manufacture. They produce the explosives that give the Malazan military its technological edge.

blue moranth.name: Blue Moranth
blue moranth.description: The Moranth clan with naval capabilities, operating ships and providing maritime support.

green moranth.name: Green Moranth
green moranth.description: The Moranth clan providing aerial transport via quorls, enabling rapid troop deployment.

gold moranth.name: Gold Moranth
gold moranth.description: The Moranth clan with diplomatic and trade functions.

silver moranth.name: Silver Moranth
silver moranth.description: A Moranth clan with specialised roles.

nah'ruk.name: Nah'ruk
nah'ruk.description: The short-tailed aggressive breed of K'Chain Che'Malle that launches a devastating attack on the Bonehunters. Their battle is one of the most intense combat sequences in the series.

shi'gal assassin.name: Shi'gal Assassin
shi'gal assassin.description: An elite K'Chain Che'Malle aerial assassin caste, capable of flight and extraordinary speed. They are used for targeted killing missions.

k'ell hunter.name: K'ell Hunter
k'ell hunter.description: The K'Chain Che'Malle warrior caste, swift and deadly reptilian fighters with blade-arms. They are the primary soldiers of the K'Chain Che'Malle.

ve'gath.name: Ve'Gath
ve'gath.description: Massive K'Chain Che'Malle heavy warrior breed, the armoured tanks of their military. They are enormous and devastating in close combat.

matron.name: Matron
matron.description: The ruling queen of a K'Chain Che'Malle nest, enormous and highly intelligent. She produces all other K'Chain Che'Malle through birthing.

gunth mach.name: Gunth Mach
gunth mach.description: A young K'Chain Che'Malle Matron-in-waiting who bonds with the human Kalyth. She represents a new generation of K'Chain Che'Malle seeking alliance with other races.

ranag.name: Ranag
ranag.description: A large, prehistoric herbivore native to the ancient plains of the Malazan world, resembling a massive rhinoceros-like beast. Ranag roamed in herds across the tundra and grasslands during the age of the Imass, hundreds of thousands of years before the present era. They were hunted extensively by the Imass alongside other megafauna such as ay (wolves) and bhederin. By the time of the T'lan Imass, ranag were already becoming rare, driven toward extinction by overhunting and the changing climate caused by Jaghut ice magic. They appear in the prologue of Memories of Ice, where the Bonecaster Pran Chole observes three ranag trapped in a boggy sinkhole — a bull male, his mate, and their calf — mired and vulnerable to predators. The scene serves as a meditation on extinction and the eternal struggle between hunter and hunted. Cannig Tol and Pran Chole discuss how the Imass hunted the ranag until they were no more, a recognition that their people were wasteful and thoughtless in their hunting. The ranag symbolise the deep ecological history of the Malazan world and the consequences of unchecked predation.
